title: unable to pause bootstrap5 carousel
body:
Hi, I am trying to pause a bootstrap 5 carousel, so that I can then play it when images have been loaded.
The carousel is created in html, and so far, in the console, I have tried
```
$('#carousel_id').carousel('pause')
$('#carousel_id').carousel({pause: true})
```
but neither of these work.
```
var myCarousel = document.querySelector('#carousel-large-background')
var carousel = new bootstrap.Carousel(myCarousel)
carousel.pause()
```
when I examine the carousel element after calling the pause method, the private property _isPaused = true, but the carousel doesn't stop.
Ideally, I would like the carousel to not autoplay so that when I have received the webworker loaded images, I can then start the carousel cycling.
Mtia...

author: frameworker2019

title: Why does .container use --bs-gutter-width for padding?
body:
The .container has this rule: ```css .container, .container-fluid, .container-lg, .container-md, .container-sm, .container-xl, .container-xxl {​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ padding-left: var(--bs-gutter-x,.75rem); padding-right: var(--bs-gutter-x,.75rem); ``` However, typically at this point, the `--bs-gutter-width` variable is not defined, so it will fall back to `.75rem`. Then in .row we have this: ```css .row { // This value depends on a SASS variable, I think. The front-end people set this to 1.5rem for a project I work on.   --bs-gutter-x: 1.5rem;   margin-left: calc(var(--bs-gutter-x)*-.5);   margin-right: calc(var(--bs-gutter-x)*-.5); ``` This is meant to compensate the padding on columns: ```css .row > * {     padding-left: calc(var(--bs-gutter-x)*.5);     padding-right: calc(var(--bs-gutter-x)*.5); ``` If you now have a .container within a .row, e.g. for a nested full-width section, the padding will be off: https://codepen.io/donqi/pen/BamWyRZ ```html

Starting the container

Starting a column

Container inside a full-width banner

``` So: - If .container is outside any .row, then using the `--bs-gutter-x` variable has no effect. - If .container is inside full-width section inside a .row, then using the `--bs-gutter-x` variable will cause mismatch with outer containers. - If a .container is used in any other place, that's probably wrong :)

title: Bootstrap, direct DOM manipulation & Vue's virtual DOM #310
body:
Hi there, 

This topic is w.r.t using Bootstrap (not just CSS, but JS components) in general with virtual-dom based frameworks like Vue & React. 

I would like to ask a very fundamental question, surprisingly I haven't really seen this discussed anywhere online expect for this stackoverflow [comment](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65547199/using-bootstrap-5-with-vue-3#comment125680515_66386443)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73772701/157210617-f40dae7e-d044-4769-923b-743e88fec64e.png)

I know mixing Vue (virtual dom) + Jquery/Vanilla JS (that does direct dom manipulator) is an [anti-pattern](https://forum.vuejs.org/t/how-vue-2-0-work-with-jquery/3591/3), [anti-pattern](https://forum.vuejs.org/t/when-is-it-ok-to-directly-manipulate-the-dom/45818/3). 

Can anyone advise how you are using bootstrap JS components with Vue/React. If not, can we assume & establish that only the CSS part of Bootstrap is safe to use with Vue/React? 

I was also wondering about wrapper libraries like https://github.com/bootstrap-vue/bootstrap-vue/discussions/6931 & [bootstrap-vue-3](https://github.com/cdmoro/bootstrap-vue-3/discussions/310), if by design, they got some smarts to avoid direct dom manipulation by bootstrap.min.js. I have posted this same topic in their respective repos, but thought I will ask the question for any users here familiar with these wrapper libraries. 

Sorry, if it turns out to be a silly question, it's quite possible I might have misunderstood somethings! Thanks in advance.

title: CSS variables in the source code become SCSS in npmjs
body:
Hi, I noticed that there are CSS variables in the source code which become SCSS variables in production.
Here is an example in the [source code](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/33827d24a4f6a4c8446584d7740c338e35b41b91/scss/_variables.scss#L1432) :
`$alert-border-radius:           var(--#{$prefix}border-radius) !default;`
And here when getting bootstrap via `npm install`
`$alert-border-radius: $border-radius !default;`

May I ask you what program does this transformation and why it happens? Would appreciate any references to the doc!
author: okadurin

title: What's reason to mass replacing SCSS variables to CSS variables?
body:
I'm seeing a massive replacement of SCSS with CSS variables in components. What caused these changes?

CSS variables doesn't look that impressive in **rendered** CSS.

pros:
- dynamic assign in CSS file
- inline assing in HTML (i.e. bad practice as this called in HTML manuals)

cons:
- slow render, uses more memory/CPU
- harder to debug/support
- less readable SCSS
author: WinterSilence

title: Breaking changes in the "main" branch by using CSS variables?
body:
Let's say I'm using bootstrap@5.1.3 and there is a piece of code like this:
```
@import "~bootstrap/scss/bootstrap";

.alert.alert-warning {
  border-width: $alert-border-radius * 2;  
}
```
It works on 5.1.3 because `$alert-border-radius` is a SCSS variable: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1a6fdfae6be09b09eaced8f0e442ca6f7680a61e/scss/_variables.scss#L1451

Then when switching to the `main` branch the piece of code above is going to fail with error:

```
SassError: Undefined operation "var(--bs-border-radius) * 2"
```
That is because the same variable now contains a CSS variable value: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/b165f35f7808b59f4e09ec518caa441e30830fbb/scss/_variables.scss#L1432
----
To mitigate this I'd need to update my code to use `calc`:
`border-width: calc(#{$alert-border-radius} * 2);`  

The question is: is it expected? I understand that this is just a `main` branch and not something which has been released. But I'd like to understand how it's going to be in the future. Will migration be required?

author: gertraudu

title: replace popper with floating ui to enable complete module bundling
body:
Popper is deprecated. The new version of popper is called FloatingUi. Bootstrap could replace Popper with [FloatingUI](https://github.com/floating-ui/floating-ui) (from the popper maintainers) and eliminate the last barriers (?) to use bootstrap as a module. 

[Taken from the docs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/getting-started/javascript/#using-bootstrap-as-a-module): 
> Due to browser limitations, some of our plugins, namely Dropdown, Tooltip and Popover plugins, cannot be used in a <script> tag with module type because they depend on Popper.

### Why?

Module support, smaller bundle size, and last but not least, using popper in module frameworks like Svelte [leads to issues](https://github.com/svelte-add/svelte-add/issues/31#issuecomment-985880894). 

### Additional information
- https://github.com/shoelace-style/shoelace/discussions/601 just migrated from Popper to Floating UI too.

title: SASS @use instead of @import
body:
I was digging thru the docs and came across [https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/customize/sass/#importing](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/customize/sass/#importing) where it uses `@import`. 

I'm not sure if this is CSS or SASS,  but if it's using SASS, the SASS docs indicate that `@use` should be used instead of `@import` [https://sass-lang.com/documentation/at-rules/import](https://sass-lang.com/documentation/at-rules/import).

It might be a good idea to explain why in the bootstrap 5 docs?
author: TravisHi

title: Add additional color utility classes similar to tailwind
body:
# Introduction I recently began using Tailwind in a new project and have been very pleased thus far. Yet, overall, I can see that I am losing a lot of speed and convenience compared to the bootstrap. One thing I noticed that I often felt missing since Bootstrap 2 was a way to customize the colors easily. I often felt constrained by the choice of only a few color groups (white, dark, success, primary, warning, and error), yet I didn't want to overwrite them. I wish I could color certain components easily while adhering to some loose form of color schema. Tailwind solved this very nicely with their utility classes. # Examples ```ruby bg-gray-50 # background in gray intensity 50, light gray bg-blue-100 # background in blue intensity 100, less light blue bg-sky-900 # background in "sky" intensity 900, pretty dark blue. border-blue-100 border-sky-200 text-fuchsia-400 dark:text-red-500 # set the text color to red in darkmode ``` Even further, these colors can be extended with a nice set of state modifiers: ```ruby hover:bg-red-500 focus:border-green-200 active:text-purple-800 dark:hover:text-white # set the text color to white in dark mode on hover ``` These can also be applied to certain borders only: ```ruby border-l-sky-300 # border left in color sky intensity 300 border-r-sky-300 # border right in color sky intensity 300 border-x-sky-300 # border right,left in color sky intensity 300 border-y-sky-300 # border top,bottom in color sky intensity 300 ``` ## A theoretical button example This enables one to create a custom button quite easily while being somewhat harmonic in terms of colors. ```html
What a nice custom violet button
``` # Simplicity Personally, I do not like the large numbers used and I see no reason why it shouldn't be something like "text-blue-5" instead of "text-blue-500". ```html
What a nice custom violet button
``` ### Why don't you write css instead? That is certainly possible, but I would most likely be forced to select a new set of colors each time. I do like the large but limited variety of colors given by Tailwind.  # Conclusion I am not a big fan of writing Tailwind since the difference between plain CSS and Tailwind is too minimal. Also, in Tailwind, I am forced to fix many browser quirks that are already solved with bootstrap. Yet, these color classes provided me with a very nice value-add compared to plain CSS. Therefore, I think they might be the right thing to be included in bootstrap as a new feature. There is no reason to remove the current color classes either; they could live side-by-side. `text-success` and `text-green-700` # Tailwind documentation https://tailwindcss.com/docs/background-color https://tailwindcss.com/docs/border-color https://tailwindcss.com/docs/text-color
author: stillhart-at-bechterew

title: Bootstrap Icons <i> tag versus <span>
body:
It is not semantically suggested to use `<i>` tag for an icon, for the following reason:

>The `<strong>`/`<b>` and `<em>`/`<i>` tags have exactly the same effect in most web browsers, but there is a fundamental difference between them: `<strong>` and `<em>` have a semantic meaning whereas `<b>` and `<i>` only convey styling information like CSS.
>
>While `<b>` can have simply no effect on a some devices with limited display or when a screen reader software is used by a blind person, `<strong>` will:
>
>Display the text bold in normal browsers
>Speak with lower tone when using a screen reader such as Jaws
>Consequently:
>
>in order to convey semantics, the `<b>` and `<i>` tags shall never be used,
>in order to convey styling information, the `<b>` and `<i>` should be avoided and CSS should be used instead. 

Is there any reasoning behind providing Bootstrap Icons on https://icons.getbootstrap.com/icons/ with an `<i>` tag instead of for example a `<span>`?

Yes, it is perfectly possible to do that when using the Icons in HTML, but it would be easier to just copy it with the proper HTML entity instead of having to change it each time, and I also think it would make the web a better place if the "big" ones (Bootstrap in this case) would just show the examples with the right tags by default.

Please apologise if I somehow am wrong with above thinking approach.

author: gerardnico

title: How to use Bootstrap 5 with Vite?
body:
How to use Bootstrap 5 with Vite? If you don't know what Vite is:

> Before ES modules were available in browsers, developers had no native mechanism for authoring JavaScript in a modularized fashion. This is why we are all familiar with the concept of "bundling": using tools that crawl, process and concatenate our source modules into files that can run in the browser.
> 
> Over time we have seen tools like [webpack](https://webpack.js.org/), [Rollup](https://rollupjs.org/) and [Parcel](https://parceljs.org/), which greatly improved the development experience for frontend developers.
> 
> However, as we start to build more and more ambitious applications, the amount of JavaScript we are dealing with also increased exponentially. It is not uncommon for large scale projects to contain thousands of modules. We are starting to hit a performance bottleneck for JavaScript based tooling: it can often take an unreasonably long wait (sometimes up to minutes!) to spin up a dev server, and even with HMR, file edits can take a couple seconds to be reflected in the browser. The slow feedback loop can greatly affect developers' productivity and happiness.
> 
> Vite aims to address these issues by leveraging new advancements in the ecosystem: the availability of native ES modules in the browser, and the rise of JavaScript tools written in compile-to-native languages.
-- https://vitejs.dev/guide/why.html

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6342102/161825624-19d87191-ae96-44e1-a955-864929a67652.png)


I've used Vite with Tailwind and it is FAST. Is it possible to get some documentation on how to use Bootstrap 5 with Vite? Thank you

When using a bootstrap 5 nav bar and viewing on mobile, clicking the "hamburger" icon opens the mobile menu but it also makes the containing div as tall as the mobile menu.  I'd prefer that not happen.  Seems to be a default behavior of bootstrap nav bar.

title: Scrollspy activation event won't fire, but still works as expected, when [data-bs-spy="scroll"] added to the body element
body:
I need to utilize the scrollspy activation event in order to have images next to the sticky scrollspy navigation change based on which link has the active class.

I made it based on this part of the documentation: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/components/scrollspy/#events

When I attached the [data-bs-spy="scroll"] to the body element, the activation event did not work, and there was no error message in the console to indicate what was causing this. However, it still functioned as if there was no issue.

I made two Code Sandboxes to demonstrate my issue, where the scrollspy activation event is set to trigger a console log of "Hello World".

This one is where the [data-bs-spy="scroll"] is in the body element: https://6xlzt6.csb.app/

This one is where the [data-bs-spy="scroll"] is in the section element containing the desired scroll section: https://w3sx5b.csb.app/

When I did add the js code to the first code sandbox with the [data-bs-spy="scroll"] in the body element, CodeSandbox did send me an error that the addEventListener equaled null.

Has anyone else encountered an issue like this and was able to solve this?

I mainly want the [data-bs-spy="scroll"] in the body element in order to avoid issues with having a separate scrolling section from the main scrolling of the body.

I tried having a div encompass all of the stuff inside the body tag with the [data-bs-spy="scroll"], but that lead to the issue in which the links got the 'active' class where their linked container ended, as if it was a step behind. title: React: autoprefixer: Replace color-adjust to print-color-adjust.
body:
Hi I try to make my first React app by some youtube tutorial. I have troubles with bootstrap. I install it, import it in the same way like tutorial do, but there  is the error autoprefixer: Replace color-adjust to print-color-adjust.. I found  here a  nice solution by @vineetdigit , but  Im pure  beginner and  I dont  understand what  author means with: "\rm -rf node_modules" and "restart the react app with "react-scripts start" " Thank you for your advice and your leniency.

This most certainly didn't work for me.

`npm install autoprefixer@10.4.5 --save-exact`

But following helped remove the warning.

In the package.json of the top level directory of the react app, add the following section after the dependencies: {..}

`"overrides":{
    "autoprefixer": "10.4.5"
  }`
delete package-lock.json
\rm -rf node_modules
npm install
restart the react app with "react-scripts start"
The warning is no more.

title: How to override component-level CSS variables?
body:
I’ve just installed the 5.2 beta as excited to try out CSS custom properties (variables)! I seem to be misunderstanding how they work, though.

In cards, I can see there are properties like `--bs-box-shadow`. So, I added a `<style>` tag with the following rule:

```html
<style>
body {
    --bs-card-box-shadow: 0 5px 10px black;
}
</style>
```

This doesn’t seem to have any effect, though. If I inspect a card on my page, it doesn’t seem to be picking up my defined value and instead has a “blank” value:

![Screenshot 2022-05-16 at 15 53 46](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/167312/168621764-b94fdbd1-97ed-48e0-a36b-a8f683cc0772.png)

![Screenshot 2022-05-16 at 15 53 34](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/167312/168621613-b289ee5b-7a6b-4568-8221-46f317cfc370.png)

It _does_ work if I define the value in a `.card` selector:

```html
<style>
.card {
    --bs-card-box-shadow: 0 5px 10px black;
}
</style>
```

But this to me defeats the point of having variables if we then have to define component-specific variables in many selectors?

I’m asking as I’m trying to introduce theming to an application and I’d like to just be able to set a load of custom properties on the `body` element (overriding Bootstrap’s set in `:root`), but this isn’t going to be feasible if I have to define property values in various CSS class selectors.

Why do property values set in a selector like `body` not propagate down to the components? Why do I have to define property values in component-specific selectors like `.card`?

author: rpayanm

title: Why not "defer" bootstrap javascript in <head>?
body:
With "async" and "defer" options for controlling when javascript is loaded, why does Boostrap documentation still recommend placing the bootstrap <script> tag  before the closing body tag </body> (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/getting-started/introduction/)? It seems like antiquated advice. The "defer" attribute tells the browser to execute the javascript after the document is loaded and parsed (they wait if needed), right before DOMContentLoaded. According to (https://caniuse.com/script-defer), 97% of all browsers support "defer".  

As stated in this thread (https://stackoverflow.com/questions/56105230/bootstrap-4-scripts-best-location-and-scripts-tags), "Head is for external resources. To have all dependencies out of the way at the start of the document is best practice. The `<head>` was made for exactly that. As the DOM is not loaded at that moment in page loading, this will fail all references to the DOM made there. There used to be a (dark) time, before defer, where it was common to get the advice to place the script-includes after `</body>` but before `</html>`."

This:
```
<head>
...
<script defer src="assets/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"></script>
</head>
```

Instead of this:
```
...
<script src="assets/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

title: How was the defaults of $container-max-widths decided?
body:
I am splitting the breakpoint of xs into an additional smaller class, xxs, via a custom.scss file:

```
$grid-breakpoints: (
    xxs: 0,
    xs: 320px,
    sm: 576px,
    md: 768px,
    lg: 992px,
    xl: 1200px,
    xxl: 1400px
);
```
I also want to add a corresponding xs size to $container-max-widths. I would like to know the rationale behind the default max-widths (see below) so I can apply it to my custom xs size. What would you recommend? 

Default _variables.scss file:
```
$container-max-widths: (
  sm: 540px,
  md: 720px,
  lg: 960px,
  xl: 1140px,
  xxl: 1320px
) !default;
```
For now, I am approximating the size by multiplying the ratio of $container-max-widths to $grid-breakpoints of the sm size (540/576= 0.9375) with my xs value (320*0.9375=300) to come up with the following for my custom.scss:

```
$container-max-widths: (
  xs: 300px,
  sm: 540px,
  md: 720px,
  lg: 960px,
  xl: 1140px,
  xxl: 1320px
)
```
Does this seem logical?

title: _carousel.scss /* rtl:begin:ignore */
body:
### Prerequisites

- [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed issues
- [X] I have [validated](https://html5.validator.nu/) any HTML to avoid common problems
- [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Describe the issue

Hi Team, you use in _carousel.scss /* rtl:begin:ignore */, but when I use RTLCSS on Bootstrap.scss it fails on "rtlcss: found end "ignore" without a matching begin." It looks it's because RTLCSS doesn't like inner directives.

```
/* rtl:begin:ignore */
    /* rtl:begin:ignore */
    /* rtl:end:ignore */
/* rtl:end:ignore */
```

Any idea how to resolve that.

Commented // @import '~bootstrap/scss/carousel'; it works.
Uncommented it doesn't work.

Windows

### What browser(s) are you seeing the problem on?

Chrome

### What version of Bootstrap are you using?

v5.1.3
author: chovanjo

title: Using CSS Grid with Bootstrap? Is it time, yet?
body:
I've been watching a few videos on the wonders of the newer CSS Grid layout module available in modern CSS. Boostrap, since v5.1.0, offers an opt-in experimental CSS Grid system. While I would like to start using CSS Grid in my designs, I am hesitant because mobile visitors have to use the latest versions of their browser for it to work. For example, https://caniuse.com/css-grid lists Firefox 101 (for android) as the earliest version that supports CSS Grid, and it was just released in May of this year. Unless I am willing to provide a fallback for just-out-of-date browsers, it still seems premature to use CSS grid?

Are you using CSS Grid yet in your responsive website designs for mobile devices? Or, is it still Flexbox all the way?

title: Bootstrap 5 Scrollbar shakiness when hovering over elements (tabs)
body:
This problem has manifested in various ways since Bootstrap 4.   But lately I'm seeing it especially on a page that I have that has tabs for displaying additional content below some header type information.  (Tested on Chrome 102.0.5005.63 and Edge) 

The problem happens if you size the vertical height of the browser to 1154px or so (just enough to get the scrollbar to show) and then use the mouse to hover over the tabs (tabs 2 and 3 and back to tab 1)  (I set my screen dimensions in F12 debug by sizing the screen to 1500x1154) 

I created a code pen to illustrate this:
[(https://codepen.io/snmoore/pen/jOZXdgP)](https://codepen.io/snmoore/pen/jOZXdgP)

It's evident that the scroll bar is appearing and disappearing, but unnecessarily as the selected tab hasn't changed from the hover.  It's just changing the hover highlighting a bit.    The hover doesn't appear to add any vertical margin or padding, so it seems odd that it is causing the scrollbar to hide and show.  

Anyone have some insight on this?

But suppose I want to change the `$primary` theme variable to a value from these built-in theme colors, like this:

author: kdb13

title: [Brainstorming]: Optimization - Get rid of unused css.
body:
Let's all agree that having unused code in production is terrible, no one likes it, and it is a waste of resources; It will affect the loading time of a web page since the browser is required to fetch more data than needed. Therefore, there should be a way to pull only the necessary data upon request. However, implementing such a mechanism is a bit challenging, hence posting this in a discussion. 

Right off the bat, I can think of having an api that handles requests and returns only the css elements needed for consumption. For instance, say we have the following endpoint: `https://bootstrap.com/css/?elements=`, a user can pull specific css elements like btn, btn-primary, mg-3, p-5, and the request becomes:

`https://bootstrap.com/css/?elements=btn,btn-primary,mg-3,p-5`

Another solution I can think of is tearing down the huge `bootstrap.min.css` file into multiple small files, say one file called `common.css`, which contains definitions for common css elements like `:root`, `@media` queries, and things of that nature. This way, a user can pull specific css files stored on edge, cdn... For example, if a user wants to use `btn`, then they have to specify the name of the file in the head tag like the following:

```html
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.1.3/dist/css/common.css"
/>
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.1.3/dist/css/btn.css"
/>
```

Another solution for this is to manually download and store the `bootstrap.min.css` file and adjust its content accordingly. However, doing such a task is hell since it requires scanning around 10k lines of css. I know that there are tools to automate such tasks. However, such tools will remove media queries and such; take the following as an example: 

![Screenshot from 2022-07-21 08-54-19](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/62179149/180146571-9dc1f206-b849-4b4d-8637-782461be965e.png)

Chrome coverage tool reported it as unused css. Therefore, it is not helpful to use such tools cause of false positives.

That is all i can think of at the moment. What do you think about such a problem?

title: Monochromatic mode
body:
### Prerequisites - [X] I have [searched](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) for duplicate or closed feature requests - [X] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Proposal ## Monochromatic themes Why does the Bootstrap framework even need to set colors? Let the developer select the color they want, and Bootstrap only manages the contrasts between the components. For DarkMode: ```html
``` ### Motivation and context 1. Reduces the cognitive load on the developer (a) Developers already know the names of colors, so they wouldn't need to use Bootstrap specific keywords "Primary", "Secondary", "Tertiary", "Dark", "Light", "navbar-dark", "navbar-light", "text-bg-light", "bg-light", etc. (b) Developers only need to add the color to the parent component, and child components below would inherit the color. 2. Reduces the code and maintenance of Bootstrap. Instead of managing "Dark", "Light", "Primary", "Secondary", etc. Bootstrap would only manage the contrast.

title: Modal option to not append to document.body?
body:
A change from 5.1.3 to 5.2.0 for modal checks if the document body contains the modal element, however if the modal element originates from within a shadow-dom `document.body.contains` will not work.

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/commit/92e664c921ad6b39d9addd7d991a88f3ea6ea7bc

The bootstrap css is loaded within the shadow-dom, so when the modal is append to document.body, it loses the css styling because the bootstrap css is encapsulated in the shadow-dom not in document scope, this causes issues where the modal won't show up because of missing css or other odd behavior.

Is there some option in the Modal class to ignore appending to document.body, or specify another root?

author: Allmedialab

title: Utilities to warkaround calc() limits
body:
Hi folks,

There are lots of questions on Stack Overflow, how to do something in pure CSS that can't be achieved due to `calc()` limits.

To give you just one example, https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38617025/using-n-inside-calc (Duplicates: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/28002684/is-there-a-way-to-use-the-n-value-in-nth-childn, https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42434460/index-of-nth-child-within-the-css-rule and so on, hundreds of votes in total).

What if create utility classes to workaround this? For instance, after adding `indexed` class to an element you would get a variable `--item-index` (or similar named) for each child of the element, that could be used in `calc()`. Implementation is trivial, but it seems to be in demand.

Another example: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/8720931/can-css-detect-the-number-of-children-an-element-has (383 votes). After adding a class, say, `count-children`, BS could set a variable `--children-number` to use within `calc()`.

Sorry, if the idea doesn't belong to Bootstrap domain.

Regards,

author: krosoftware

title: Inconsistent behavior of Scrollspy after 5.2 update
body:
Dear all,

tl;dr: Scrollspy in 5.2 highlights different items depending on scroll direction, in particular items above the screen when scrolling up.

I'm currently updating a project from 5.1.3 to 5.2.0 and I can't get Scrollspy to work in the same way. I have a page which has two DIVs next to each other. The left-hand side DIV contains the NAV menu (table of contents), the right-hand side DIV contains an HTML document. The HTML document (pulled from a database) can become quite lengthy with many sections, so the table of contents may become so long that both DIVs are scrollable. Consequently, the "spy" is not on the body, but on the DIV with the actual content.

With 5.1.3, the two DIVs were set up as:
`<nav id="navbar-minutes" class="navbar navbar-light>`

And:
`<div class="containter-meeting-minutes" data-bs-spy="scroll" data-bs-target="#navbar-minutes" data-bs-offset="160">`

Respectively. This worked consistently with 5.1.3 (at least in Chrome and Safari) that when scrolling either way, whatever section was at the top of the right-hand side DIV, was highlighted in the NAV menu on the left. Also, when clicking on a link in the NAV menu, the right-hand side scrolled to the right position, and again the corresponding on the left was highlighted correctly.

With 5.2.0, I have changed the the second DIVs definition to:
`<div class="containter-meeting-minutes" data-bs-spy="scroll" data-bs-target="#navbar-minutes" data-bs-root-margin="160px 0px 0px 0px">`

But the behavior is very different:
- When scrolling down, whatever section just enters at the bottom of the DIV gets highlighted in the table of contents, instead of when it reaches the top. So when I click on a section, and it is a shorter section (more than once section visible on the screen), it may happen that the highlighting in the NAV menu is one or two items below from where I clicked.
- When scrolling up, the section that is highlighted is the one that just enters the top of the screen, even before it enters the top of the DIV.
- When scrolling up and down alternately in an area of the document where there are three short sections visible on the screen, the highlighting in the table of contents jumps up and down by 4-5 menu entries at once, because it jumps from the section at the bottom of the DIV when scrolling down to the section at (above) the top of the DIV when scrolling up, and vice versa.

Through trial and error, I have now set it to:
`<div class="containter-meeting-minutes" data-bs-spy="scroll" data-bs-target="#navbar-minutes" data-bs-root-margin="160px 0px -90% 0px">`

This probably breaks the system since I've just moved the bottom margin to very close to the top of my DIV. But at least this gives me very close to the intended behavior when scrolling down, either by scrolling or by clicking on a table of contents entry further down. The section that is highlighted by scrollspy now consistently is the one at or near the top of the right-hand side DIV. WooHoo!

But the highlighting still is all over the place when scrolling up. The section that gets highlighted is always above the visible area. 

It feels like the margin isn't applied when scrolling up, only when scrolling down.

Example:

After scrolling down, the section at the top of the screen is correctly highlighted:
![scrolling_down](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66065605/184544685-26a061ff-c379-43c7-8e3d-d38f3ca6d290.png)

After scrolling up (here by clicking on the menu item highlighted in grey), the section highlighted by scrollspy is two items above. The corresponding section is well above the visible area.
![scrolling_up](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66065605/184544715-782bf380-be59-4c79-b65e-4b6e4086d4de.png)

Note: the DIV the scrollspy is on is the white area on the right. The 160px offset/top margin accommodates the fixed headings. 

Maybe I'm doing it wrong. Maybe I'm using it for something it's not intended for. In any case, I'd appreciate any ideas. Thanks!

author: terabytesoftw

title: Activatable button
body:
Greetings,

I would like to propose an addition to the button groups that we could for now refer to as an **activatable button**. 

The idea of an activatable button is inspired by emergency buttons which have a protective cover like this:
https://www.securitysafetyproducts.co.uk/images/upload/products_image5-11545-d.jpg

The button is off by default but cannot be accidentally clicked until activated. I have made a rough example here:
https://stackblitz.com/edit/3tkzv2-8fjkd4?file=index.html

The advantages of working with an activatable button are:
- no need for confirmation modals, whose action can be anywhere on the page (violation of Fitt's law);
- safety: no fear of accidentally triggering whatever action is behind the button as it is off by default;

It may be improved by using a switch instead of a checkbox or simply a checkbox to activate.

I don't think I've seen this before but I'm happy to be proved wrong.

title: Bootstrap 5.2 : should btn-link have a gradient?
body:
Hello, Following the buttons refactoring in v5.2, there is a change of default behaviour on the `btn-link` buttons, which I find quite undesirable, and have not found a native way to neutralise. Basically, from 5.2, all `btn` buttons have a gradient defined, and applied when compiling with `$enable-gradients: true;`. That was not the case in 5.1.3, so even if gradients were enabled, only the buttons with background (`btn-primary`, `btn-secondary`...) had the gradient and `btn-link` was left with a "flat" design, which I think is more appropriate. To reproduce the error, I have the minimal following code. **SCSS**: ```scss $enable-gradients: true; @import "../node_modules/bootstrap/scss/bootstrap"; .testarea { background-color: tan; } ``` **HTML**: ```html

Bootstrap 5.2


``` When running with Bootstrap 5.1.3, I get the following output (with gradient on Dark and no gradient on Link): ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5150782/185803266-14e2dddd-5245-4647-9e39-3b1958217ada.png) When running with Bootstrap 5.2.0, I get the following output (with gradients on both Dark and Link) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5150782/185803207-a414123e-dce3-44cc-951f-8c30958793e9.png) I have not found a flag in Bootstrap to restore the previous behaviour or apply the gradient more specifically, and could only think of applying the following in my custom SCSS after importing Bootstrap. Should I stick with this "hack" or raise an issue? ```scss .btn-link { background-image: none; } ```
author: jon48

title: Bootstrap 5.2 - How to toggle a dropdown using javascript?
body:
Hi, I feel I am missing something obvious and simple, but I just can't figure this out. I want to control a dropdown using javascript only (not using data attributes) but it's not behaving the way that I would expect. [The dropdown docs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/dropdowns/#via-javascript) clearly state: 

> data-bs-toggle="dropdown" still required
> Regardless of whether you call your dropdown via JavaScript or instead use the data-api, data-bs-toggle="dropdown" is always required to be present on the dropdown’s trigger element.

The problem is that as soon as I add `data-bs-toggle="dropdown"` to my dropdown trigger element, the dropdown works without using javascript (ie: adding the `data-bs-toggle="dropdown"` attribute is causing the dropdown to be controlled via data attributes which I do not want).

I've tried removing the `data-bs-toggle="dropdown"` which then allows me to control using javascript, although that causes yet another problem in which the default `autoClose` behavior no longer works so I can only close the dropdown by clicking the trigger element again (and I would like to be able to close by clicking anywhere else on the page like it does while using the data attribute method). I haven't had time to dig into the bootstrap source code to see how this all works behind the scenes, but I suspect this broken `autoClose` behavior may be due to the missing `data-bs-toggle="dropdown"` attribute.

Below is a simple example demonstrating my problem. The line of javascript to toggle the dropdown has been purposely commented out to show that the dropdown works without it. And if you remove the `data-bs-toggle="dropdown"` the dropdown will not work any longer until you uncomment the javascript, but then cannot autoClose.

[jsfiddle](https://jsfiddle.net/q6w3vb5r/)

What am I missing here? Is this a bug or am I just misunderstanding how to control a dropdown using javascript?

title: Why are breakpoints the building blocks of responsive design?
body:
Bootstrap's documentation states that breakpoints are the building blocks of responsive design. However, I don't quite understand why this is so. For example, I have a navbar that I want to 'shrinkwrap' to the inherent width of the content, and break into a burger menu only when the viewport gets too narrow to display the navbar at that width: ![navbar](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/793830/190827375-c3ef5b8f-353a-4a7b-97db-6b74d74bbb61.png) My understanding is that I can't really achieve this with Bootstrap, and that it's not designed to let me do it. The navbar is supposed to collapse at a higher width that its 'shrinkwrapped' width, when there is still some black space to the right of it. Why is this a better way to do responsive design? To underscore what I'm talking about, look at the following navbar. It's styled like this: ```
  • Home
    • [...]
``` Because of `d-inline-flex`, it shrinkwraps so the background nearly fits the content, which is desired. But Bootstrap either makes me break at a wider width than the navbar, like 992px with `.navbar-expand-lg`: ![navbar1](https://i.stack.imgur.com/xOdZl.gif) ... or not break at all, displaying a horizontal scrollbar instead of breaking just when the viewport gets too small: ![navbar2](https://i.stack.imgur.com/GeBkK.gif) Neither of these are desirable to me. Why doesn't Bootstrap cater for breaking to a burger menu at the natural content width?

title: AdminLTE vs Pure Bootstrap for a new web project?
body:
I'm primarily a freelance backend dev and for the first time venturing on full-stack development of a non-trivial web app on my own, hence I needed some guidance.

I've got all the backend stuff in php/mysql covered, I just want to know what's the best way to create a dashboard (with left sidebar) considering various aspects like long-term code maintenance and support, robustness, etc. Looks don't matter that much as it's a CRUD app but obviously, better is more appreciated.

Based on research until now, [AdminLTE](https://adminlte.io/) seems to be the most popular way of doing it among most devs although a few others like material and coreui also seem to have some street cred.

But another approach I'm considering apart from AdminLTE is to just use pure bootstrap and fiddle up my own sidebar [using something like this](https://bootsnipp.com/snippets/Q0dAX). That way, I won't be tied to just one Bootstrap version which is used by AdminLTE (v4.6) and troubleshooting will be much easier through google search and StackOverflow. What do you guys think is the right approach?

title: How should responsive display classes be overriden using Javascript?
body:
If we use [`d-none`, `d-sm-inline` and so on](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/utilities/display/) how can we get this to work nicely with legacy components that use `$.hide()` or similar?

The `!important` declaration for the `d-*` classes mean that `$.hide()`, `$.show()` and anything that sets `display:none` don't work.

Are there other ways of responsive showing/hiding of content? Am I misunderstanding the applicability of the display utilities? I thought it was a convenient way of showing/hiding content according to screen size but we also need to be able to remove/add components at runtime too.

I notice the same issue is there with the Collapse component - it doesn't seem to work with `d-*` which feels a bit odd - shouldn't out-of-the-box components work together?

Hello, Everyone!

Recently I created a new Bootstrap 5 UI Kits for my next project.  Almost Bootstrap components are implementd by Atlassian Design.  In addition to Bootstrap’s default component, I also created new components that often used in projects: Avatar, Blankslate, Steps(Progress Tracker), Sidebar+Menu.

Demo:  https://fastbootstrap.com/
Download: https://github.com/fastbootstrap/atlassian-design-for-bootstrap

## How to migrate from Bootstrap 5 ?

It's very simple, just use our new `fastbootstrap.css` instead of `bootstrap.css`, then refresh your page to see the result! 

If you trying use our extened components(Javascript Plug), use `fastbootstrap.js` instead of `bootstrap.js`.

Waiting for your feedback.  🙏

author: rohith2810

title: :focus-visible not supported by all old browsers
body:
Hello all,

I am currently working on focus for Boosted and I have a problem with the PR "Rework button focus/active styling, with extra changes for checks/radios #37026".

@patrickhlauke switched from ':focus' to ':focus-visible' arguing that ':focus-visible' is now supported by most browsers.

According to [https://caniuse.com/?search=focus-visible](https://caniuse.com/?search=focus-visible) it is mostly true.

But for Firefox 4-84, it is mentioned that it needs the prefix -moz-focusring.
According to [https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/:-moz-focusring](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/:-moz-focusring ), this prefix is non-standard and is not on a standards track so we should not use it on production.
In addition, it may not work even after adding the prefix -moz-focusring as I tested on Browserstack.

For Chrome, it seems not supported by default too.

Howewer if we refer to [.browserslistrc file](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/v5.2.2/.browserslistrc) Bootstrap is supposed to support old browsers (e.g. Firefox 68, etc.).

So what to do here?

Stop using ':focus-visible' since it's not supported by all old browsers?
Keep using it and add a fallback for some browsers like Firefox 60 to 84?
Keep using it and update the list of supported browsers on [.browserslistrc file](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/v5.2.2/.browserslistrc)?

Best regards.
Mewen

title: separate files for headers and footers
body:
I am in the process of updating my Bootstrap v3 site to v5.  

In v3, I separated common elements such as the header, navbar and footer into three files.  These files were then imported into each of the content pages using Server Side Includes (SSI).  The advantage is that one only has to maintain one header + menu file and one footer file across the complete site.  At that time I used #include statements and the header and footer files were saved as .shtml.

For example:

```
<body>
    <!--#include virtual="../templates/KYM-banner.shtml" -->
    <!--#include virtual="../templates/nav_Main_EN.shtml" -->
```

Is there a different more efficient recommended way to do this in Bootstrap v5, one that is also compatible with CDNs such as Cloudflare?  I can't seem to find any info on the bootstrap site in this regard, which is surprising given that this would seem to be a fairly common need, or am I missing a totally different current approach?

Thank you, in advance...!

title: Can Bootstrap SCSS be placed in a CSS Cascade Layer?
body:
Hello,

With [CSS Cascade Layers](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Cascade#basic_example) now supported in all major browsers, I'm interested to know if there is a known or recommended technique to using them with Bootstrap?

TLDR - I'm mostly wondering if there are major blockers in using Cascade Layers + Bootstrap + SCSS and if it's even worth pursuing today.

--- 

What I'd like to accomplish is placing different groups of styles into different layers, including Bootstrap's generated/included styles from SCSS.

However, I haven't seen any documentation or examples indicating this is even possible.

It appears [support is being worked on](https://github.com/sass/sass/issues/3285) but there are also references to [a recommended approach for layers and SCSS](https://github.com/sass/sass/issues/3240) that focuses on mixins vs partials.
author: seangwright

title: Bootstrap 5.1/5.2 Introduce CSS variables, but they don't work in emails.
body: I recently upgraded to the latest Bootstrap (5.2), using the ruby gem (v5.2.2), which uses CSS variables (I think that's a cool idea for the future), however, I do not believe that this is supported in email clients (at least not in Gmail), and so my email templates are semi broken. Anything that references the global or root CSS variables doesn't work. For example, while testing locally using the Rails preview feature, I see the CSS variables assigned and present: However, in a deployed environment, when I send the email to an actual inbox, those same variables are missing, even though they're being used: I believe that Gmail is stripping them out because I can see in the code of the original message the following: ``` ----==_mimepart_637663751e9e1_2ef2501b88846e Content-Type: text/html; charset=iso-8859-1 Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable ``` so Gmail is receiving these values in the email content the app is sending, but because Gmail strips the `html` tag altogether, they are lost. but even if I tried to move the CSS variables to an element other than the `html` tag, Gmail strips them. I was able to hack a solution by spending a long time manually re-creating the main mixins and classes that I needed for the email using the SASS variables instead of CSS variables, so for example, I had to manually override this mixin: ```scss @mixin button-variant( $background, $border, $color: color-contrast($background), $hover-background: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($background, $btn-hover-bg-shade-amount), tint-color($background, $btn-hover-bg-tint-amount)), $hover-border: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($border, $btn-hover-border-shade-amount), tint-color($border, $btn-hover-border-tint-amount)), $hover-color: color-contrast($hover-background), $active-background: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($background, $btn-active-bg-shade-amount), tint-color($background, $btn-active-bg-tint-amount)), $active-border: if($color == $color-contrast-light, shade-color($border, $btn-active-border-shade-amount), tint-color($border, $btn-active-border-tint-amount)), $active-color: color-contrast($active-background), $disabled-background: $background, $disabled-border: $border, $disabled-color: color-contrast($disabled-background) ) { color: #{$color}; background-color: #{$background}; border-color: #{$border}; &:hover { color: #{$hover-color}; background-color: #{$hover-background}; border-color: #{$hover-border}; } &:active { color: #{$active-color}; background-color: #{$active-background}; border-color: #{$active-border}; } &:disabled { color: #{$disabled-color}; background-color: #{$disabled-background}; border-color: #{$disabled-border}; } } ``` and others like it. This is obviously not maintainable for the future and I might have to do more customization in the future if I end up using a component I didn't already fix. So my question is: is there a way to use Bootstrap 5.2 without CSS variables? Or does anyone know of an easy way to compile Bootstrap and replace all of the `var(--bs-foo)` or `var(--#{$prefix}foo)` with the actual values? Or has anyone gotten the new version of bootstrap to work in email templates?

title: Offcanvas accessibility question
body:
Hey there, 

I'm quite a noob on accessibility but I wanted to have some other point of views. I feel like this question might be answered by a11y experts since it's more a question of accessibility but feel free to discuss about the subject in this thread.
I was using aXe to debug some a11y issues and I found two things overall:

- Offcanvases are labelledby their title but they don't have any specific role. My question here is: should we introduce a role on those divs ? Or maybe should offcanvases be inside `<section>`s instead of `<div>`s ? So I got this issue: 
```sh
aria-labelledby attribute is not well supported on a div with no valid role attribute.
```

- My second question is related to the Bootstrap documentation itself, I can see that the left sidebar menu (which is the main navigation of the docs) is inside an `<aside>` element. But as far as I can see, the `<aside>` element should be used to add something indirectly related to the main content (according to [MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/aside)). So here's my question: Is there any reason I'm missing to have this tag wrapping the main Bootstrap navigation ? Should we remove the aside element to only have the offcanvas markup (with or without a `<section>` element) ?

If any answer is yes or if you don't see what I mean by this, I could open a PR on one/both subject(s).
Thanks a lot for your time !

``` **Offcanvas case:** ```
...
``` **Temporary solution** ``` const modals = document.querySelectorAll('.modal.modal-local') modals.forEach((modal) => { modal.addEventListener('shown.bs.modal', event => { const backdrop = document.querySelector('.modal-backdrop') modal.parentNode.insertBefore(backdrop, modal.nextSibling) }) }) ``` OR editable Modal.Default properties??: ``` import {Modal} from "bootstrap" Modal.Default.rootElement = Modal.Default._element.parentNode ```

title: Why is there no [skip ci] or [ci skip] keyword in commit messages after 2017?
body:
Hi folks!

I was using GitHub Search API for a research study and realized that [skip ci] or [ci skip] keyword, which can make the workflow runs skipped for the commit whose message includes one of these keywords, suddenly disappear after 2017. I checked some versions of the "Contributing Guidelines" with the hope to find some rules about that. However, I could not find anything. 

By the way, you can see [skip ci] or [ci skip] keyword's behavior from the following GitHub documentation link: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/managing-workflow-runs/skipping-workflow-runs

Is there anyone who might know the reason for this sudden disappearance? I think there should be a reason to change the developer's behavior about that.

title: How do I deal with sticky :hover on iOS?
body:
There used to be a section about sticky `:hover` and `:focus` on iOS in the docs, but it seems to have disappeared as of Bootstrap 5. However, it's still a huge problem: buttons hold the `:hover` state until you tap on something else. What's more, they don't actually enter that state until you either let go or hold the button for about a second. Seems like the thing we want is for buttons to go into the hover state as soon as the user touches the button, and remove that state as soon as touches end or are cancelled.

Am I stuck implementing this in JavaScript? It seems like a huge problem and I can't imagine no-one on the Bootstrap project has noticed it. It happens with all of the buttons on the [example pages](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/examples/heroes/). It just seems weird that there used to be information about it and now that info is gone, but the problem still exists. Everything I can find about it seems to heavily imply (but not outright state) that it's not an issue anymore… can anyone help me out here?

author: markddrake

title: Destroying dynamic popovers/tooltips and memory leaks
body:
The popover, and other opt-in components such the tooltip, is associated with a DOM element. When creating one dynamically (e.g. when used in a SPA), and thereafter removing it, it should be first destroyed via [`dispose()`](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/components/popovers/#options).

What happens if that DOM element is deleted without destroying the popover object first? Would that result in a memory leak?

(I wonder whether the browser automatically cleans up such objects. Most tutorials and StackOverflow code I've seen doesn't bother with disposal, but intuitively that feels wrong to me.)

title: Photo gallery
body:
Several times in frontend development, we were faced with the need to create a photo gallery for our projects.

A solution I developed was using the Bootstrap fremawork and its components. With the use of lightbox and jQuery it is possible to create a fully responsive photo gallery.

The version used to implement the solution is Bootstap 4.0.0, but you can use any version that uses the lightbox plugin.

The solution does not need to install any additional frameworks or plugins.

See the working solution here: https://bpgazola.github.io/bootstrap-photo-gallery/gallery.html

To download: https://github.com/bpgazola/photo-gallery-bootstrap.git

title: Why flex-shrink 0 for all columns?
body:
Hi all,

When the v5 grid system was introduced (#28517), `position: relative` was removed because it was felt that it didn't make sense to impose that for all columns:

- #25254

Does anyone know why the default [flex-shrink](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/flex-shrink) value (of 1) was overridden with `flex-shrink: 0` for **all** columns[^1]. See below for relevant excerpts of #28517.

Thanks!

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1299b163c7526b0e6892856d87e8710bc195d7f5/scss/mixins/_grid.scss#L16-L28

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1299b163c7526b0e6892856d87e8710bc195d7f5/scss/_grid.scss#L12-L18

[^1]: It's actually added to all `.row` children via `.row > *`.

title: Form validation with form-floating
body:
version 5.2 and above has interesting form input called floating labels which looks a lot like material (android). There is no example in [floating labels document](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.2/forms/floating-labels/) to show both error and label on top of the text. ![Invalid Input](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2040435/215253959-54114e5c-e517-4c5b-b091-2d851bf74336.jpg) Currently I'm doing this ```css input.is-invalid ~ label.invalid {display:inline !important;} input.is-invalid ~ label.label {display:none !important;} input:not(.is-invalid) ~ label.invalid {display:none !important;} input:not(.is-invalid) ~ label.label {display:inline !important;} ``` ```html
``` Is there a ready to use bootstrap CSS to achieve this ? Of course I still need a javascript to verify input validity but that is out of scope of this question.

title: Destroy all lists in nav and dropdown menu!
body:

Please consider destroying all lists in nav and dropdown menu.

title: row-cols and wrapped columns outer margins
body:
Been trying to figure this out for awhile now, and just not making any headway on it. Bootstrap 5.2.3 If I setup 5 cards, each within its own column and use the row-cols of the encompassing row to specify different column allowances based on screen size... (eg row-cols-lg-3) is there a way to get a margin above the wrapped columns ? I'm trying to post example code, but it keeps dropping all my div commands, ```html
...blah card 1
...blah card 2
...blah card 3
...blah card 4
...blah card 5
``` So here is what is happening in that scenario: The row-cols-lg-3 will take the first 3 columns supplied and display them in the first on-screen row of the row. The remaining two columns are wrapped to the next row on the screen (as they should be). BUT... my row has mt-2 specified. The FIRST row on the screen (not wrapped) shows the mt-2. The WRAPPED row slams itself up against the bottom of the first row without maintaining the mt-2 above it. I've tried surrounding the cards with separate divs specifying m-2 or p-2. I've tried adding p-2 to the columns themselves. NONE of that opens the mt-2 space above the wrapped row. I mean... I could recode it separately for sm/md/lg+ screens, but that sort of defeats the purpose of being responsive. I hope that makes sense, without the sample code it is hard to explain.

title: Bootstrap Offcanvas integration with CKEditor 5 (related to z-index, focus, backdrop and body scroll)
body:
Hi everyone!

I am using CKEditor 5 WYSIWYG editor with Bootstrap.
CSS collision between CKEditor 5 and Bootstrap makes me totally lost...

CKEditor official docs shows some solution about that.
I changed `z-index` related to CKEditor balloon tooltip, making it correctly! (initailly never appeared on screen for hours...)
(https://ckeditor.com/docs/ckeditor5/latest/installation/frameworks/css.html#bootstrap-modals)

But when I click the CKEditor's tooltip icon, the focus is automatically(forcefully) moved on close button on Bootstrap offcanvas!!
I don't know how to handle this :(

If that's more CKEditor-specific problem sorry for a wrong place.
But it can be a general case for any other 3rd party WYSIWYG library which provides with a kind of balloon tooltip I think.

Any suggestion about this problem will be really helpful for me. 
Thanks for reading this question!! (Big thanks to this wonderful open source project too!)


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/108768169/222499443-84ac81b0-72e7-4b96-abad-a2dfd8a60506.mp4


(more information on this...)

After I changed offcanvas to be body scrollable and no backdrop, ckeditor worked correctly
(`data-bs-scroll="true"`, `data-bs-backdrop="false"`).
However, if I changed offcanvas backdrop to static, the original problem happened again... :(

Is there a reference to mechanisms and css of offcanvas with various option(such as body scroll, backdrop)?
Current docs doesn't make me understand why this situation happens.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/108768169/222628608-c74578cc-1f0c-4adc-87f6-cf7cec44a5a8.mp4

title: BS5 Nested dropdown display border-radius to all .dropdown-item
body:
After disabling the $dropdown-padding-y and creating a multi-level dropdown, I observed that the border-radius is applied to all my .dropdown-items. **Here is my code:** ```
  • Home
  • Tools
  • Features
  • Pricing
    • About Us
    • How it Works?
      • Blog
      • Guides & Tutorials
      • Help Center
      • Get In Touch
      • Community
``` **Here is a simple test:** ``` .dropdown-menu { > .dropdown-item:first-child, > li:first-child .dropdown-item { color: red; } > .dropdown-item:last-child, > li:last-child .dropdown-item { color: green; } } ```

author: Frank-D

title: Creating a collapse instance shouldn't also toggle the element
body:
I find the collapse feature in BS5 to be much less pleasant to use than in previous versions. I've just spent quite a lot of time trying to work out why my collapsible elements were immediately toggling when a collapse instance was created. I was doing this:

````
const myCollapse = new bootstrap.Collapse("#myCollapse");
````

expecting to subsequently be able to call

````
myCollapse.show();
````

when I needed to show the content (which is after another element is clicked, not immediately). I couldn't work out why the element was being shown immediately until I eventually realised that that is _by design_, which seems very unintuitive to me. I finally understood that the example in the [docs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/collapse/#methods):

````
const bsCollapse = new bootstrap.Collapse('#myCollapse', {
  toggle: false
});
````
isn't really a suggestion (i.e. "You can pass the `toggle` option if you want to"), it's a requirement if you don't want the element to immediately be shown.

To me it would be much more intuitive for the basic initialisation with no options parameter to do nothing but return a collapse instance, and require the state to be set explicitly. I.e:

````
const myCollapse = new bootstrap.Collapse("#myCollapse"); // Get the collapse instance
myCollapse.show(); // Show the collapse element
````
or perhaps
````
const myCollapse = new bootstrap.Collapse("#myCollapse").show();
````

At the very least, the documentation should be absolutely explicit that creating a collapse instance will also cause it to be toggled to visible unless an options parameter is passed to prevent it.

author: stoachup

title: table-striped-{rowspan}
body:
I have some tables where the information is stacked. For example, a name and some data over an address. With this, there are two rows per entry. This makes table-striped a bit awkward, especially if it is more than two rows per entry, thought that is rare.

I have simulated striping rows with a bit of code along the lines of:
`<tr<?php if($i%2==1) echo  ' class="table-secondary"';?>>`

However, the table-secondary is different from the table-striped color. If I use them on the same page in different tables this becomes more evident.

It would be nice if there was a way to tell table-striped how many rows to consecutively do in one color. Then, row blocks could be striped the same way, you could emulate old 3 and 3 striping, etc. The exact command could be in the form of:

table-striped-{rowspan}
    or 
table-striped span-{rowspan}

Or something similar, where {rowspan} would be the number of rows before changing colors.

Another solution would be access to the table-striped colors via the table color sets. Then, my manual striping could be made to match the rest of the page.

author: davideluque

title: NOT filter icons
body:
Right now, if I want to look for an icon, I can search on the page or filter. Searching on the page is problematic because I do not always guess what you might name things. Filtering allows for a slighly broader set of results even though it has the same issues. How? Because of tags and groups.

If I take 0 circle fill, I see these:
Tags: number, numeral
Category: Shapes 

If I go for Airplane:
Tags: flight, flying, plane, air, airport, aircraft
Category: Transportation

GREAT! The problem is, what I want 99.999% of the time is NOT to see all the Brands. It is great to have them, but I do not need the logo for Andoid, AMD, Discord, etc. unless I am doing something related to those Brands. I should not be using their trademarks as menu items, random art, etc. Thus, it would be very useful for you to have an advanced search or a way to use -filter to remove things. Then, a filter like -brand -transpo would get me a small but broad range of results.

Another option would be a more advanced search page where the tags and categoies are check boxes on the side. Then a person can uncheck all and then recheck just the few they need. For speed, I would not do instant changes. Wait for an apply so the person does not need to wait every time they check/uncheck one thing. They could uncheck all, recheck the 2 or three things they want, and finally apply to get the results.

title: Use of HTML native  <dialog> element for modals, offcanvas, popovers, & tooltips
body: I've been reading recently that most (if not all) commonly used browsers now support use of the native html dialog element, which is used for custom modal and popover like creation without bootstrap. My question is are there any plans for bootstrap to use this element at some point to create any of the following components: modals, offcanvases, popovers, & tooltips? some benefits to using this are that accessibility is mostly built in and interpreted by the browser, backdrop is included natively (can be styled with CSS), and escape keys automatically close a modal instances just to name a few. I've been reading over this dev's blog and he seems to have a solid understanding of the element works and goes into more detail. [Modals Will Never Be The Same - HTML dialog Element](https://blog.webdevsimplified.com/2023-04/html-dialog/) below is a basic snippet ### HTML ```html You can see me ``` ### JS ```javascript const dialog = document.querySelector("dialog"); dialog.show(); // Opens a non-modal dialog dialog.showModal(); // Opens a modal ```

author: coudrak

title: Switch out sass \w vanilla css
body:
I know, i know. Crazy idea uh?

Well, let me explain. modern css have came a long way. 
css in 2023 have built in things such a css nesting today.
So features like this is all possible 
```css
/* nesting-rules */
article {
  & p {
    color: #333;
  }
}
```
https://preset-env.cssdb.org/features/#nesting-rules
https://chromestatus.com/feature/5800613594529792

even things such as
- variables
- [color mixing](https://preset-env.cssdb.org/features/#color-mix)
- [Custom Media Queries](https://preset-env.cssdb.org/features/#custom-media-queries)
- and a lot more [new features](https://preset-env.cssdb.org/features/#nesting-rules)
- @import (yea, i know but a build process like PostCSS could bundle vanilla css)
- and support for css modules

So by using PostCSS you could get the tomorrows new feature supported today in all browsers.
PostCSS also takes care of autoprefixing things so you don't have too. it have the same browserlist functionallity. 

You _might_ not even need a build process for simply testing out things in latest chrome. Only when you want to create a production ready 

sass seems to me like something very complicated to get up and running. it's built in dart which is a completely other language than the web. and it requires a build process too. 

have a look at https://postcss.org to learn more

title: Table color/background - 5.2 -> 5.3
body:
Struggling with a change from 5.2.3 to 5.3.0 where it comes to table background color and text color.

Trying to find out the _simplest_ way to get the same output with 5.3.0 as I had in 5.2.3 (am no CSS-person - so it may well be me doing something stupid)

Using 5.2.3 I can set the background for an entire table with a bg-* class and the text color within the table using text-* on the table tag.  This meant that I could get a table to merge within a surrounding container in terms of color.

For example - let's use success as background and white as text - just to have something that shows the issue:

In 5.2.3 I could state `<table class="table text-white bg-success">` 

[5.2.3 example](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://gist.githubusercontent.com/chrissearle/680a3f39ff9e4995ba35beea594d2590/raw/2dac7b86f826df484c9c3ceeb76ae9dd4c1bc190/bs52.html)

Updating to 5.3.0 with the new color stuff - those are ignored.

I've tried setting the class to the new table-* - that does take effect - but gives me a transparent over white color and the text color is still ignored.

5.3.0 example has two tables - first with no changes - second with `<table class="table table-success text-white">`

[5.3.0 example](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://gist.githubusercontent.com/chrissearle/680a3f39ff9e4995ba35beea594d2590/raw/2dac7b86f826df484c9c3ceeb76ae9dd4c1bc190/bs53.html)

title: ng build fails with: 2 rules skipped due to selector errors:   .form-floating>~label -> Did not expect successive traversals
body:
I am experiencing an Issue after upgrading from bootstrap `5.2.3` to` 5.3.0 `in an angular` 16.1.3 `project.
When running `ng build -c=prod` these warnings appear.

```
⠋ Generating index html...2 rules skipped due to selector errors:
  .form-floating>~label -> Did not expect successive traversals.
  .form-floating>~label -> Did not expect successive traversals.
```

I cannot provide more details about the project. 
Since we don't use any .form-floating in our code my guess is that the warnings come from the bootstrap file: `bootstrap.css` where on line 2600 as well as on line 2616 there are definitions of `.form-floating > label `

Does anyone know:
1. What the problem is ?
2. How this problem can be fixed without suppressing the warnings ?

Thanks in advance

title: [4.6] padding-right: 17px; margin-right: -32px; added randomly to the elements
body:
I have an issue I don't quite understand, apart from a guess that this is due to an ugly attempt at scrollbar width compensation.

I have a container layout with 'row's consisting of a single data entry 'col' and two position:absolute buttons at the right.
The "remove" button positioned to be visually inside the data column near the right edge, and the progress spinner centered at the column right edge.
When I'm developing the dialog alone (using all the same set of library scripts), it works quite well, but once I load it into the application, the button and spinner are moved about 1rem to the right.

Inspecting the DOM, I see `padding-right: 17px; margin-right: -32px;` style added to the row element out of nowhere. The only way to work around this behavior I've found is to explicitly define margins and paddings of the row element, using px/mx classes for example. Which is quite frustrating.

Is there a better way of handling this issue?

title: Preventing Popovers from repositioning on scroll
body:
I've been working on a little CSS inspector JS project which uses Bootstrap popovers to display the CSS attributes of a property. It's generally working pretty well.

One thing I can't figure out is how to prevent the popovers from repositioning. In my case I'd like them **always** to display to the left of the element and not reposition on scroll. I've read through all [the Popover docs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/popovers/) and the PopperJS docs too.

I've searched for an answer too, but the only question on [Stack Overflow I found is unanswered](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75838513/prevent-bootstrap-5-popover-from-repositioning-on-scroll). Can anyone help?

title: Survey: Open-Source Documentation for Newcomers
body:
Hello Bootstrap Community!

We are researchers from George Mason University in the United States, looking for open-source contributors to participate in our survey on open-source software (OSS) project documentation use when onboarding. If you are 18 or older and contributed to an OSS project in the last one year, you can help!

The objective of our research is to better understand what type of documentation in OSS projects is more helpful for project newcomers. The survey will take approximately 10 minutes, and upon successful completion of the survey, you will be able to participate in a draw for a chance to win one of four Amazon.com gift cards worth $50 via email.

The link to survey: https://go.gmu.edu/Onboarding_Study

We would be greatly appreciative if you would be willing to participate in this study and help us improve the effectiveness of the onboarding to software projects.

If this message would best be directed or reposted somewhere, please let us know, and we will be happy to modify/repost it.

Thank you for considering participating in our research study!

Research Team: Nursena Kurubas, Dr. Kevin Moran and Dr. Brittany Johnson

IRBNet #: 2029296-1 (Shows that we have a permit for human-involved user studies)

title: The font size in the component should be calculated based on css custom variables
body:
All components involving font size (including h1-h6) should now be calculated and designed on the client (browser) through css custom variables (--bs-body-font-size) instead of writing specific value.

It should be done based on the following as base source

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/bde23ae6612b71376e6cd906679425bd58d8db72/scss/_reboot.scss#L52

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/344e912d04b5b6a04482113eff20ab416ff01048/dist/css/bootstrap.css#L199


Below are some specific local examples, without giving too many examples.
In addition, where em is used as the unit, I am not sure whether it needs to be changed.

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/344e912d04b5b6a04482113eff20ab416ff01048/dist/css/bootstrap.css#L225-L266

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/344e912d04b5b6a04482113eff20ab416ff01048/dist/css/bootstrap.css#L520

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/344e912d04b5b6a04482113eff20ab416ff01048/dist/css/bootstrap.css#L604



The reason is that when the basic font size becomes larger or smaller, the corresponding components should also change accordingly, so that it will not cause misalignment or display problems.
Generally, components do not change after being enlarged, because in the client, some sizes have been given specific values ​​instead of subsequent calculation changes. Although this step will consume the resources of the client, it should not be changed frequently.

https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/ce114117a8710016022c40e717cb1f3ec95a5d7f/scss/_reboot.scss#L246

title: Change breakpoint class convention.
body:
I'm here because I was creating a library that sorts classes based on the structure of classes. I ran into a problem.
I was trying to use a regex to break apart the strings for this library. 
```js
/^(?<type>[a-z]+|[a-z]+-[a-z]+)(?<breakpoint>-[a-z]+)-(?<value>[a-z0-9]+)(?<state>-[a-z]+)?$/
``` 
But it could not work at all. Because of this conflict 

```txt
 bg-primary-hover
 bg-md-primary-hover
```

This group `(?<breakpoint>-[a-z]+)`  will capture. both **md** for  `bg-md-primary-hover` and **primary** for `bg-primary-hover`.

In order for me this test to pass you need to have a symbol in front of md. 
I suggest `@` as in `@md`  but it's your choice. 

## Context 

I'm trying to create a library that removes classes with duplicate class types and different values from the string.
It's an alternative to tailwind merge that is supposed to be `framework agnostic`. But due to the many frameworks out there 
I have to pluck out different kinds of class strings using different methods. 

This library uses `clsx` to evaluate classes then it breaks them apart using an object with maps.
The maps only contain one class type `bg` `g-col` state `-hover` `-focus`  and breakpoint `-md`.
If two classes fits one or more of these conditions the one that comes later stays the one that comes after goes away.  
Finally after the classes are sorted the map is used to reassemble the classes that were broken into the map.
 
If you want to learn about my project or this problem in depth please look into.
[cn-efs](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@code-fixer-23/cn-efs)
[tailwind-merge](https://www.npmjs.com/package/tailwind-merge)

title: Include -xx-unset & -xx-inherit classes
body:
There are times it is useful to apply a setting on sub-breakpoint elements but not above.
For example, to apply an order on flex items below a breakpoint but not above.
This is particularly useful on navbar menus where you might want some items to remain on the bar after collapse for example.
In this case, I would apply "`order-first order-lg-unset`" to the navbar-nav. 
`order-first` moves the item before the collapsed menu so that it remains on the navbar whether or not the collapsed menu is shown
`order-lg-unset` removes the order-first and returns the item to its default ordering above the lg breakpoint.

Similarly for inherit
`ms-2 ms-md-inherit` would apply a start margin on small screens but revert to the inherited value above the md breakpoint.

  • Contact
``` ## Expected Behavior I expect the button to display borders from the btn-outline-light class while also aligning text as per the nav-link class. ## Actual Behavior Borders are not visible when both nav-link and btn btn-outline-light classes are applied together. ## Attempts to Resolve Checked for any conflicting CSS rules. Ensured that the Bootstrap version is the latest and properly linked. Searched through Bootstrap's documentation for known issues. # Question Is there an inherent conflict when using nav-link with btn and btn-outline classes, or am I potentially missing something? Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

title: Automatically Remove Unused CSS and JavaScript Code in Bootstrap Builds
body:
Currently, Bootstrap includes all of its CSS and JavaScript code in the production build, even if not all of the code is used by the application. This can lead to larger page sizes and slower loading times. Tailwind CSS addresses this issue by automatically removing unused CSS code during the production build process.

We propose implementing a similar feature for Bootstrap to automatically remove unused CSS and JavaScript code during the production build process. This would improve page performance and provide a more consistent development experience with Tailwind CSS.

**Benefits:**

* **Reduced page size:** By removing unused code, the production build size of Bootstrap would be significantly smaller, leading to faster loading times.
* **Improved performance:** Smaller page sizes translate to improved page performance, providing a better user experience.
* **Consistent development experience:** The ability to automatically remove unused code would align Bootstrap with Tailwind CSS in terms of development workflow and optimization techniques.

**Implementation:**

The feature could be implemented using a tool like PurgeCSS or UnCSS. These tools analyze the HTML and CSS files to identify which CSS selectors are actually used and remove the unused ones. For JavaScript, a similar tool could be used to analyze the JavaScript code and remove unused code.

**Additional considerations:**

* **Whitelist mechanism:** To ensure that critical Bootstrap components are not removed, a whitelist mechanism could be implemented. This would allow developers to specify which CSS selectors and JavaScript code should always be included in the production build.
* **Configuration options:** The feature could provide configuration options to control the level of optimization. For example, developers might want to choose to remove only unused CSS selectors or to also remove unused JavaScript code.

We believe that this feature would be a valuable addition to Bootstrap and would help to improve the performance and development experience of Bootstrap projects.
![IMG_20231129_232844](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/59386700/d3908df4-5feb-4f2c-a777-0f35f0a2d8db)
