title: Overlapping tooltips in BS4
body:
Hello, I’ve not (yet) upgraded to BS5 but I suspect the issue may replicate for both BS4 ([ref](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.6/components/tooltips/#overview)) and BS5 ([ref](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/components/tooltips/#overview)) because both use the same [Popper](https://popper.js.org/) which received the [last update about six years ago](https://github.com/vusion/popper.js/). Given this HTML of an `` with embedded `` element ```html

A bit of strong emphasized text.

``` both of which with tooltips, I get this behavior: ![tooltip](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/12053937/dd2fc056-311a-4aa7-a330-7cab29a7d666) Configuration of the tooltips is: ```javascript $(function() { $("[title]").tooltip({"delay": {"show": 1000, "hide": 100}, "placement": "auto"}); }); ``` I have the mild suspicion that this isn’t really a BS problem but more of a Popper issue? Does anybody have any recommendations on how to handle these overlapping tooltips — other than explicitly setting the `data-placement` attribute? (That won’t work well because the elements are generated on the fly and with resizing windows even manual placement can create overlapping tooltips.)

title: It may be the right time to recommend using `@use` instead of `@import`
body:
The sass team has emitted the formal recommendation of not using `@import` anymore a long time ago but Bootstrap documentation still display examples that use this keyword instead of `@use`.

This leads to some confusion by the community since some of us recommend to the developers to use the `@use` keyword while the Bootstrap documentation recommends otherwise.

This stack overflow answer for example, written by myself, has a legit comment by someone asking which is right - Bootstrap documentation or my answer.

https://stackoverflow.com/a/77814573/354490

I think that updating the documentation in favour of `@use` will prepare the community for the eventual removal of `@import` and would additionally encourage everybody to embrace the very clean and powerful modular approach that SASS has been proposing for years and that is largely ignored as of today.

title: Confusing description of spacing utilities notation

I'm using the link here (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/utilities/spacing/). It says "Spacing utilities that apply to all breakpoints, from xs to xxl, have no breakpoint abbreviation in them. This is because those classes are applied from min-width: 0 and up, and thus are not bound by a media query. The remaining breakpoints, however, do include a breakpoint abbreviation." Could anyone please give me some guidance on what does "the remaining breakpoints" mean? And in the next paragraph, it says "The classes are named using the format {property}{sides}-{size} for xs and {property}{sides}-{breakpoint}-{size} for sm, md, lg, xl, and xxl." . Installing collected packages: django-bootstrap-v5
Successfully installed django-bootstrap-v5-1.0.11

```
# settings.py

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',

    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',

]

Django
psycopg2-binary
django-bootstrap-v5

Deprecated==1.2.14

distro==1.9.0
Django==4.2.10
django-bootstrap-v5==1.0.11

title: Dropdown menu not functioning in React project despite proper Bootstrap integration
body:
## Description:
I've integrated Bootstrap into my React project to create a navbar with a dropdown menu, following the documentation provided. However, despite importing Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript into my App.js file and ensuring proper initialization, the dropdown menu does not function as expected.

I've double-checked my code for any errors, ensured that all dependencies are properly installed, and inspected the browser console for any error messages, but haven't been able to identify the root cause of the issue.

## Steps to reproduce:

 - Create a new React project.
  - Install Bootstrap using npm or yarn.
   - Import Bootstrap CSS and JavaScript into the project's main file (App.js).
   - Implement a navbar with a dropdown menu using Bootstrap classes.
   - Run the project and attempt to interact with the dropdown menu.

## Expected behavior:
When clicking on the dropdown toggle, the menu should expand and display the dropdown items.

## Actual behavior:
Clicking on the dropdown toggle does not expand the menu, and dropdown items are not displayed.

title: Set margin-left on non-typographical elements regardless of RTL/LTR?
body:
In some cases where elements are embedded in text flow, it makes alot of sense to use the new start/end classes so that the padding or margin moves to the appropriate side when text is reordered for another language.

However, in the vast majority of cases we're adjusting spacing on layout elements that remain in the same relative positions regardless of what language is being displayed.  In trying to migrate from prior version of bootstrap, we're having challenges separating the concerns of language direction specific spacing and layout spacing unrelated to the language.  

E.g. I might have two columns side by side, and set ml-1 on the second element.  I don't want language selection to impact this particular spacing, hence why I would not use ms-1.  These columns do not get reordered based on language selection.  Using ms-1 results in the spacing being moved from between the columns to the right side of the second column and failing to achieve the desired result.  This is an oversimplified example of course.  Certainly I can work around this in a variety of ways, but it certainly feels very unnatural compared to prior bootstrap versions.

Axios Cross-Site Request Forgery Vulnerability - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-wf5p-g6vw-rhxx
"An issue discovered in Axios 0.8.1 through 1.5.1 inadvertently reveals the confidential XSRF-TOKEN stored in cookies by including it in the HTTP header X-XSRF-TOKEN for every request made to any host allowing attackers to view sensitive information."

title: Modal, Offcanvas Roles
body:
Bootstrap adds a role of dialog to both modal and offcanvas elements. The documentation alludes to including aria-labelledby or aria-labels on these elements. I understand that role probably isn't needed until the user engages with the element but this does create a conformance issue in the markup.

Can somone help me understand why bootstrap went this route? If I were to add a role to the markup, closing the element would just remove it. I'd have to add an event to all modal and offcanvas elements and that feels a bit sloppy.

Our goal is to eliminate all conformance issues and this - for years - is one that we have to leave in our reports and the explain away. It doesn't stop lawyers from harassing our clients though.

title: bootstrap tooltips displayed outside scroll area
body:
I am using Bootstrap 5.3.0. When Bootstrap tooltips are loaded within a scrollable div, the tooltips are visible outside the scroll area on initialization and during scrolling up or down.

The tooltips wont display until the Modal is fully displayed, so i had to add an event listener to catch this with 'shown.bs.modal'

I have created a fiddle to reproduce the issue https://jsfiddle.net/nmkca5v6/2/

The .tooltip class z-index is 1080 by default, but if i force change it to anything else it seems to not display at all.. the z-index for the modal i believe is 1055 but even if i set the modal z-index to a higher number e.g. 2000 then the tooltips are no longer visible.

I'm not sure how to address this issue or if it's a bug with Bootstrap. I see a similar report here https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/35202 but I dont see a solution was provided.

Expected results:

For tooltips not to display outside the scroll area during initialization and during scrolling.

title: New Complex Components
body: Hi there, I've been using this tool since version 1, so I know it is great and very versatile for all types of projects. However, I've always wondered why more complex components are not created. While looking for how I could create new ones, I found a site called [SyncFunction](https://ej2.syncfusion.com/home/vue.html?_gl=1*ehp9xs*_gcl_au*MzIxNDM5OTA5LjE3MTg4MTEwMTg.*_ga*MjAxOTc5MTA1MC4xNzE4NjgyMTg3*_ga_41J4HFMX1J*MTcxODgxNTk0Ni40LjEuMTcxODgxNjA1My4wLjAuMA..*_ga_R3Q3BGFFC9*MTcxODgxNjAzMy4xLjAuMTcxODgxNjAzMy42MC4wLjA.&_ga=2.7949173.632610207.1718816033-2019791050.1718682187) that offers a wide range of components. The idea is not to copy them, but I feel that Bootstrap has the potential and maturity to include more complex components. From my perspective, many of these examples are simple, but with each new project, they need to be rebuilt, even though they could be part of your repository for easy insertion. The idea is not to lose the concept of reusable components with these new components that we, as developers, build. Therefore, I invite you to take a detailed look at these new components, as they could greatly enhance the excellent work you have been doing. Thank you!

author: VMLuca

title: We need to add logical CSS as we have already updated utility classes
body:
Hello Team,

I was looking into the logical styles for our utilities such as **padding, margin, border-radius,** etc.

In the [utilities.scss](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_utilities.scss) file of Bootstrap, we see an example like:
```scss
"padding-end": (
  responsive: true,
  property: padding-right,
  class: pe,
  values: $spacers
),
```
Bootstrap has updated the class name from `padding-right` or `pr` to `padding-end` or `pe`, which is great. However, we also need to update the property to align with logical styling:
```scss
"padding-end": (
  responsive: true,
  property: padding-inline-end,
  class: pe,
  values: $spacers
),
```
This way, the RTL styles will work seamlessly without requiring additional RTL-specific styles.

Is there a possibility of adding this in a future update? Also, I'd like to know if there are any drawbacks to using these styling methods.

I'm looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thank you.
author: monab

title: Bootstrap Forced Colors CSS for Windows High Contrast Themes
body:
I've created a stylesheet by using the `forced-colors: active` media query which enhances the accessibility of Bootstrap 5 when using with [Hight Contrast themes in Windows](https://blogs.windows.com/msedgedev/2020/09/17/styling-for-windows-high-contrast-with-new-standards-for-forced-colors/).

- GitHub repo: https://github.com/coliff/bootstrap-forced-colors-css
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bootstrap-forced-colors-css
- Demo: https://coliff.github.io/bootstrap-forced-colors-css/tests/

Some fixes made are a bit opinionated, e.g. increasing border width from 1px to 2px on form inputs to counter the fact that there is no box-shadow.

It'd be great to see some/most of these fixes be made to Bootstrap in the future. For now though, users can use this stylesheet to improve things for Windows users with High Contrast themes.

```html
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap-forced-colors-css@1/css/bootstrap-forced-colors.min.css" 
  media="screen and (forced-colors: active)">
```

<details>
<summary>How to test with Forced Colors Mode Emulation in Chrome / Edge</summary>

![edge-test-forced-colors-active](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/1212885/dc681871-7605-42d8-82d0-b6361e7f3faa)

![edge-test-forced-colors-active-light-mode](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/assets/1212885/df7766a8-d825-4845-8a2e-1f733dbd38e8)

</details>

### Interesting links about Windows High Contrast Mode:

- [Smashing Magazine article](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2022/03/windows-high-contrast-colors-mode-css-custom-properties/)
- [CanIUse Support](https://caniuse.com/mdn-css_at-rules_media_forced-colors) (Note that currently it's Windows-only)
- [WHCM and System Colors](https://adrianroselli.com/2021/02/whcm-and-system-colors.html)

author: dafaher

title: CSP Compatibility with bootstrap.min.js
body:
Hi Bootstrap Team,

I am using bootstrap.min.js (version 3.4) in my AngularJS project. I have a Content-Security-Policy (CSP) defined as follows:
`
      <add name="Content-Security-Policy" value="script-src 'self' http: https: 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval';"/>
`

To enhance security, I need to remove **unsafe-eval** from the policy. Therefore, I need to check if the library file is CSP compatible, specifically looking for the usage of **setTimeout**, **setInterval**, eval, and Function without causing errors in the console.

**What I Have Tried :**

In the bootstrap.min.js library file (version 3.4), I noticed:
- setTimeout is used 6 times.
- setInterval is used once.

I have debugged the code, and while the first two setTimeout functions work properly, the subsequent setTimeout calls are not being executed when I remove unsafe-eval.


**Question**

Is bootstrap.min.js (version 3.4) fully CSP compatible without the `unsafe-eval` ?

**Additional Information:**

Operating System: Windows
Browser: Chrome
Bootstrap Version: v3.4.1

author: ErikThiart

title: Documentation from the perspective of newcomers
body:
Hi, when reading the [docs](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/introduction/) I have a feeling of being overwhelmed. Even though I've been using Bootstrap 4 some time ago, I wanted to refresh my knowledge and see what's different in version 5. 
Could you rethink the documentation from a perspective of someone who heard about Bootstrap and wanted to use it in their project, and has basic knowledge of HTML and JS?

Let's take a look at the [Introduction](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/introduction/#quick-start) section:

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4b4004d-e203-4742-a791-cbab326e5790)

What the user is introduced with, is a basic HTML structure for Bootstrap to work, which is good, but then, it's not really clear what to read next to actually get something nice created. There is a big **Next steps** section, but:

- "Read a bit more about some [important global environment settings](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/introduction/#important-globals) that Bootstrap utilizes" - this link leads to the section half a page below which is not really necessary, it just makes the navigation more complex
- "Read about what’s included in Bootstrap in our [contents section](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/contents/)" - this one sends to contents section which suggests that we might need more files to download to start working
- "Need a little more power?" - no, I just want to start hacking with Bootstrap first, to check if this is even for me

To be honest, the natural "Next step" should be just reading the page to the end first. I.e. why is this section in the middle of the page - it should be at the end.

Then, the user looks at the navigation sections and sees "Customize" above "Layout"
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d8ff1f84-d18a-4657-9687-04f778c9f6e0)

This strongly implies that to use Bootstrap, we might need to modify the sources.

I would suggest to move "Layout" section above "Customize", make it more clear to read about the Grid and layout in Bootstrap. Maybe even create one more page under Introduction that describes them in a concise way. Take the [w3schools tutorial](https://www.w3schools.com/bootstrap5/bootstrap_get_started.php) as an example - it feels more beginner friendly but is not an official documentation and might contain errors and now follow the latest changes.

Hope this makes some sense. Let me know what you think (maybe I'm just a noob).

title: Hamburger menu not working
body:
Why does my hamburger menu not work when I include the following tags...

In the HEAD...

`<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet">`

Just before the BODY...

`<script src="https://code.jquery.com/jquery-3.2.1.slim.min.js" integrity="sha384-KJ3o2DKtIkvYIK3UENzmM7KCkRr/rE9/Qpg6aAZGJwFDMVNA/GpGFF93hXpG5KkN" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>`

`<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@popperjs/core@2.9.2/dist/umd/popper.min.js" integrity="sha384-IQsoLXl5PILFhosVNubq5LC7Qb9DXgDA9i+tQ8Zj3iwWAwPtgFTxbJ8NT4GN1R8p" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>`

`<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.0.2/dist/js/bootstrap.min.js" integrity="sha384-cVKIPhGWiC2Al4u+LWgxfKTRIcfu0JTxR+EQDz/bgldoEyl4H0zUF0QKbrJ0EcQF" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>`

However, when I swap the last tag out for this, it works fine...

`<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@4.0.0/dist/js/bootstrap.min.js" integrity="sha384-JZR6Spejh4U02d8jOt6vLEHfe/JQGiRRSQQxSfFWpi1MquVdAyjUar5+76PVCmYl" crossorigin="anonymous"></script>`

The version 5 documentation says to include the 5.0.2 distribution in the <head> even though the current version is 5.3.0. I've tried that but it makes no odds. I've also tried different version numbers in the </body> but only version 4.0.0 works. 

Also, why am I including the bootstrap.min.js twice? Why not just in the HEAD?

And what's with the integrity property?

Some of the documentation tells me to use this in the <head>...

`<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet">`

Other places tell me to use this...

`<link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.0.2/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet" integrity="sha384-EVSTQN3/azprG1Anm3QDgpJLIm9Nao0Yz1ztcQTwFspd3yD65VohhpuuCOmLASjC" crossorigin="anonymous">`

If I change the version of the one with the integrity property it breaks, so I'm assuming it's there to verify something? However, the documentation for the 5.3.0 version doesn't have the integrity property.

In summary, if I want the latest version of Bootstrap, and I want the hamburger menu to work, what tags should I be using in the HEAD and BODY please?

Thanks in advance!

title: Django templates wrapping React app bootstrap CSS bleeding through to react components
body:
Hi there, I've got an app I've inherited with a complicated UI, it is a Django templated app, that uses Bootrap v5.3.3. However, it also wraps/pulls in a React SPA app that sits in a CDN, that doesn't use Bootrap, just raw SCSS.

What I'm noticing is that when I use a `ul` or a custom `Accordion` component for instance in the React app, it all looks great while developing that app, but when deployed and sitting inside a Bootstrap styled django app, I'm seeing some bleed-through of Bootstrap CSS being added or overriding the React app's CSS.

Of course this is bad design, but it is in maintenance mode and I'm unable to re-achitect, so I'm wondering the best way forward. I guess I'm asking the community if:

1. there is a way to ignore bootstrap in some circumstances.
2. If I added something like [normalize.css](https://www.npmjs.com/package/normalize.css/v/3.0.3) to the react app, would it prevent this bleed-through.
3. Is there anything else I should try.

There are no silly ideas on this one (ignoring the existing architecture of the app....), whatever ideas you have are welcome, thanks.

title: How to put the focus on a certain input field on a modal form using Bootstrap?
body:
I've put my first steps in Bootstrap in combination with Flask and all works well so far. I'm using Bootstrap version 4.6.2. I have an app running with the following, simple layout: ![flask_immc](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12320d3d-eca4-489b-b32b-e0d46f08d2ea) When I click on the "Add New Wiring Connection" I have a modal form popping up like so: ![addConnection](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9123de12-b5fc-4b0c-a7d0-dd57b7cf7ff9) Again, all is working fine. However, if the modal window opens I would like to have the first input field (input field belonging to "_Device name:_") to automatically have the focus and its surrounding border blueish (standard border colour when having the focus). It surprises me that Bootstrap, with such a huge and overwhelming amount of features and/or possibilities, doesn't have this at hand by default. I read [here](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.6/components/modal/) that, because of HTML5, the `autofocus` attribute doesn't work and you have to use a JavaScript to aim for the same effect. I tried this solution but it doesn't work. Most probably - if not surely - because of my still not huge experience with this framework. Below is the code I have that creates the modal form: ```

Add Connection

``` The item of interest is the `` tag, I have the following JavaScript (just as given in the above mentioned link on the Bootstrap website): ``` ``` If I use the Chrome debugger (pressing F12) and put a breakpoint on the variables then I see the variables are getting the correct element assigned by the `document.getElementById()` call. I've also put a breakpoint on the line that should set the focus: `$('#myFocusedInput').trigger('focus')`. When I open the modal form then that breakpoint is hit (I also see the log text on the Chrome debugger console). But the first input field itself is still not having the focus (_the cursor is not put automatically in that input field_ nor is the border of the input field blueish). Result: my first input field on the modal form is not getting the focus I want it to have and the cursor is not put automatically in that first input field so that the user can start typing right away without having to select that first input field first. Can somebody point me to the problem in the code? Best,

title: Setting WIDTH and HEIGHT for images causes image shrinking with padding/borders
body:
In order to reduce content shifting on page loading with several embedded images, many are now advising we go retro and add explicit WIDTH and HEIGHT attributes to IMG tags for responsive images. The problem when doing this with Bootstrap is that as soon as we introduce any padding or borders to the images, the WIDTH and HEIGHT attributes end up defining maximum size of the image **plus** the padding/borders, rather than just the image itself, hence shrinking the image to satisfy the WIDTH and HEIGHT attributes. This does not occur when defining margins - only when introducing padding and borders. Refer to the screenshot included below ![screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8efb8374-09f2-4579-804a-267c9b2b4393) The simple code to test this is below. title: Bootstrap VS Code Extension
body:
I've been thinking about how great it would be to have an official Bootstrap VS Code extension. Some of the features I thought of that it could have:

- **Autocomplete CSS classes** - this one's a no-brainer to include. I know this is already possible with some other extensions like https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=Zignd.html-css-class-completion but would make sense for it to be included.
- **Lightweight linting of HTML templates** - [Bootlint](https://github.com/twbs/bootlint) is deprecated now, but it used to be useful for catching HTML issues with Bootstrap component. Could be good to resurrect some of the rules from this.
- **Autocomplete values for `data-bs-` HTML**. e.g. type`data-bs-t` and it'll suggest autocompleting to `data-bs-toggle` (I guess some of this is already available via AI extensions like GitHub Copilot, but not everyone has access to that)
- **Warnings in console about using deprecated features / classes**
- **Automatically remove redundant CSS classes from templates**, e.g. "col-9 col-md-9" is replaced with "col-9" - like https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=AgustinFornio.redundanty (great plugin by the way!)
- Autosuggesting updating older version CDN links. e.g. if you are loading https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/bootstrap@5.3.0/dist/css/bootstrap.min.css - it could show a note in the console that 5.3.3. is available (maybe could right click on it to auto-fix!)
- All the above could be configurable in the options.

I've done maintenance of [VS Code extensions](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=HTMLHint.vscode-htmlhint) before but I don't have enough experience/knowledge to build this myself from scratch. Maybe it could be a community effort? I'd be happy to help contribute :-)

title: Grid and .row, layout breaking
body: Hi all, While looking at our Drupal sites, which are utilizing Bootstrap 5.3, I came across an issue with the layout breaking when using some elements with some x styles/attributes in the main content area (I haven't really been able to put my finger on it). Our pages are arranged using grid like: | `div.sidebar.sidebar-first.col-md-3.order-md-first` | | `main.col.main-content` | | `div.sidebar.sidebar-second.col-md-3.order-md-last` | What happens is for some types of content in the main-content area of the page, like we use a Paragraph bundle for image slideshows which are responsive and will size down as needed, it breaks the layout so that it looks like: | `div.sidebar.sidebar-first.col-md-3.order-md-first` | | `main.col.main-content` | | `div.sidebar.sidebar-second.col-md-3.order-md-last` | After hitting my head on my desk for a while trying to figure out what exactly is breaking this layout, I found that if I use the following CSS then everything appears as it should: ``` .row > * { min-width: 1px; } ``` with my CSS tweak: without my CSS tweak: For what it's worth, changing the selector from `.row > *` to `main#content` also looks to fix the issue. Few questions... 1. Has anyone else encountered this? 2. Is this maybe something that is missing from Bootstrap out-of-the-box? 3. Does anyone see any issues with using this in a production environment? I haven't come across anything that visually stands out on any page. Thanks in advance! :)

title: Responsive containers that stop at the breakpoint
body:

So to show some code:

`.responsive-container-lg` will behave like a normal `.container` until the lg-breakpoint is reached and then doesn't scale up further.

How could this be implemented? The current logic for generating the container classes with sass is quite complicated to understand for me.

See https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/scss/_containers.scss for the current code

author: WelkerAcero

title: What is the reason for the special treatment of `fixed-*` and `sticky-*` elements when the scrollbar disappears during `modal.show`?
body:
When using a modal, the scrollbar is removed from the body, and padding is added to compensate for the scrollbar width, ensuring the layout remains consistent. This functionality is handled by [Bootstrap's `scrollbar.js`](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/main/js/src/util/scrollbar.js).

However, there is also special treatment for elements with `sticky` or `fixed` classes. I’m trying to understand why this is necessary, as everything seems properly aligned with just the padding on the body.

I can’t identify a layout scenario where this special treatment would be required to prevent alignment issues.

Thank you.

title: img-fluid does not work when wrapped inside flex-shrink-0
body:
Hey! Bootstrap is great! I am using "Media + Object" layout from [here](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/utilities/flex/#media-object). So my image is inside `flex-shrink-0` and details/text inside `flex-grow-1`. However `img-fluid` does not work (wider images don't shrink on small media). - Like this, the media + object idea works (image column takes as little width as possible), `img-fluid` doesn't work: ```html
{% if photos.0 %} {% endif %}
``` - Wrapping img inside `container`, `container-fluid` or `container-md` does not make image responsive either: ```html
{% if photos.0 %} ``` - Like this, `img-fluid` works, but it breaks the "Media + Object" idea (`flex-shrink-0` doesn't work, narrow images leave a lot of blank space on the side on wider screens. ) ```html
{% if photos.0 %} {% endif %}
``` **Is there a combination of BS classes that would make `img-fluid` work inside `flex-shrink-0`?**

title: Status of Bootstrap as a project?
body:
Hello,

I’m just wondering what the status of Bootstrap is? As the last release (5.3.3) was exactly a year ago now.

I’ve been a regular user of Bootstrap since v1, help out with the Discord and Reddit communities, and been a supporter/defender of Bootstrap in the wake of tribal-like supporters of other CSS libraries; but it’s getting a bit difficult to defend Bootstrap as a viable solution for creating website and web app front-ends when—from the surface—Bootstrap looks dead for lack of a better word.

I appreciate this may come across negative and demoralising, but that’s not my intention. It comes from a place of love of Bootstrap and wanting to see Bootstrap flourish in 2025 and beyond. So would be keen to find out who’s actually working on the project, the roadmap for Bootstrap, and ways in which I can help.

title: bootstrap graphic folio site | image loading issues
body:
Good morning Github

I am a visual and physical creator with minimal coding know-how that has somehow been enough to put together a folio site of my work using bootstrap [www.joejo.com](http://www.joejo.com/) . . . somehow

I have very limited understanding of the terms and language around coding to communicate this effectively – I appreciate your tolerance of this limitation

Yesterday I saw that images were not displaying correctly for the first time since I built and launched the site, maybe 8 years ago [Bootstrap v3.3.5]

My observations:
- The issue seems to be linked with the downloading and display of any type of image on any page, including static page headers for example, not just 'onmouseover' images.
- It seems related to some kind of limit for images to download and display fully. If the 'Reload this page' button at the top of a browsers' window is clicked repeatedly, all the unloaded images will eventually download and display, the number of times the 'Reload this page' button needs clicking for all the images to load and display, seems to be linked to the number of images on the page, ie: fewer images fewer clicks, more images more clicks. Performing a 'hard refresh' or 'force reload' using the keyboard 'Ctrl + F5' results in perpetuating the problem, with missing images and empty placeholders.
- I’ve had confirmation from IONOS that it's not related to hosting issues
- I’m aware as I look at this that the Bootstrap version used is very old

I'd value any thoughts, insights and suggestions. Thank you. Joe.
author: joejo70

title: How I Successfully Installed Ruby on Rails on Debian 12 Without Breaking My ISPConfig Setup
body:
Here’s the updated guide with the anchor text **"Install Ruby on Rails on Debian 12"** naturally integrated into the content:

---

**How I Successfully Installed Ruby on Rails on Debian 12 Without Breaking My ISPConfig Setup**

Hey everyone,  
I recently struggled to *Install Ruby on Rails on Debian 12* for my ISPConfig 3 server, and after a lot of trial and error, I finally got it working without messing up my existing websites. I thought I’d share my steps here to help anyone facing the same issue.

---

### **My Problem**  
I needed to *[Install Ruby on Rails on Debian 12](https://docs.vultr.com/how-to-install-ruby-on-rails-on-debian-12)* for a specific ISPConfig site, but all the guides I found either didn’t work with ISPConfig or risked breaking my existing setup. I also wanted to run the app in a subdirectory and access it via `xxxxx.com:3000`.

---

### **The Solution**  
Here’s how I did it:

---

#### **Step 1: Install Ruby and Rails**  
First, I installed Ruby and Rails system-wide but made sure it didn’t interfere with my existing websites.  

1. **Update the system:**  
   ```bash
   sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
   ```

2. **Install dependencies:**  
   ```bash
   sudo apt install curl gnupg2 dirmngr git-core zlib1g-dev build-essential libssl-dev libreadline-dev libyaml-dev libsqlite3-dev sqlite3 libxml2-dev libxslt1-dev libcurl4-openssl-dev software-properties-common libffi-dev
   ```

3. **Install Ruby using rbenv (to avoid conflicts):**  
   ```bash
   curl -fsSL https://github.com/rbenv/rbenv-installer/raw/main/bin/rbenv-installer | bash
   echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.rbenv/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.bashrc
   echo 'eval "$(rbenv init -)"' >> ~/.bashrc
   source ~/.bashrc
   rbenv install 3.2.2  # Replace with the version you need
   rbenv global 3.2.2
   ```

4. **Install Rails:**  
   ```bash
   gem install rails -v 7.0.4  # Replace with the version you need
   rbenv rehash
   ```

---

#### **Step 2: Set Up the Rails App in a Subdirectory**  
I wanted to run the app in a subdirectory of my ISPConfig site. Here’s how I did it:

1. **Create a new directory for the app:**  
   ```bash
   mkdir /var/www/xxxxx.com/rails_app
   cd /var/www/xxxxx.com/rails_app
   ```

2. **Generate a new Rails app (or clone your existing app):**  
   ```bash
   rails new . --database=mysql  # Replace with your database choice
   ```

3. **Configure the database:**  
   Edit the `config/database.yml` file to match your database settings.

4. **Run the app on port 3000:**  
   ```bash
   rails server -b 0.0.0.0 -p 3000
   ```

---

#### **Step 3: Configure ISPConfig to Allow Access to Port 3000**  
By default, ISPConfig doesn’t allow access to custom ports like 3000. Here’s how I fixed it:

1. **Open the port in the firewall:**  
   ```bash
   sudo ufw allow 3000
   ```

2. **Create a reverse proxy in ISPConfig:**  
   - Go to your ISPConfig panel.
   - Navigate to the site you want to use (e.g., `xxxxx.com`).
   - Add a new subdomain or use an existing one.
   - Set up a reverse proxy to forward requests from `xxxxx.com/rails_app` to `localhost:3000`.

---

#### **Step 4: Test the Setup**  
Once everything is configured, visit `xxxxx.com:3000` or `xxxxx.com/rails_app` to see if your Rails app is running.

---

### **Final Thoughts**  
If you’re trying to *Install Ruby on Rails on Debian 12* with ISPConfig, this method worked perfectly for me, and I didn’t have to worry about breaking my existing websites. I hope this guide helps you avoid the headaches I went through!

This question is about, and only about, editing software for Bootstrap 5.

I'm thinking of resuscitating my website. In the past I've used both Joomla, Wordpress and Bootstrap 3 but I wrote my own html5 and css for my most recent website. I thought I do the final version [I'm 76 and ought to know better] in BS 5 which obviously differs somewhat from 3. The main problem I have is finding a code editor. I used to use Homesite 5 until it was phased out. In spite of that I continued to use it and Dreamweaver but they became unreliable at rendering accurately in browsers so I gradually moved to Atom which now also seems to have been scrapped. So the hunt is on for a decent editor - decent = something better to use than notepad!

I have looked at Visual Studio, Phoenix, Blue Griffon, Pinegrow,  and Silex and basically hate them all and for much the same reason. I don't want to have to spend hours and hours learning how to use what is essentially a text editor. Something that works out of the box, without having to install this, that and the other to make it work will be ideal. My OS is W10.

The website itself is essentially dull. Static pages of text with images and the occasional bit of video. It needs to be responsive but other than that I am not interested in impressing anyone with popovers, graphic effects etc. The sooner I can get back to playing the cello and reading William Dunbar ["Done is a battell on the dragon blak…" that Dunbar] the better.

And suggestions gratefully received!

author: spart4ck

title: why dependency track consider bootstrap version 5.3.3  affected by CVE-2024-6484 ?
body:
Regarding Depenedncy track tool bootstrap v 5.3.3 is affected. A vulnerability has been identified in Bootstrap that exposes users to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. The issue is present in the carousel component, where the data-slide and data-slide-to attributes can be exploited through the href attribute of an <a> tag due to inadequate sanitization. This vulnerability could potentially enable attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript within the victim's browser.
Does version  5.3.4 comes with a fix ?

author: abielmagudev

title: Bootstrap Update and Roadmap – April 2025
body:
Hey Bootstrap community!

We know things have felt a bit quiet lately. That’s mainly because the core team works on Bootstrap in their spare time—and sometimes, that time just isn’t there. But despite that, we’ve made meaningful progress, especially on Sass compatibility issues.

---

## ✅ v5.3.4: Sass Deprecation Warning Fixes

One of the main updates in **Bootstrap v5.3.4** (and the quick follow-up **v5.3.5**) was to **resolve the Sass deprecation warnings** introduced in **Sass v1.77.7**.

We’ve patched our code to prevent these warnings from showing up when using Sass versions up to **1.78.0**. If you stay on that version or below, you shouldn’t see any deprecation warnings.

📝 **Note:** Sass deprecated several features starting in 1.77.x. They will become breaking in Sass v2.

---

## 🔧 If You're Still Seeing Warnings...

If you’re compiling Bootstrap Sass and seeing deprecation warnings, here are your options:

- **Pin your Sass version to 1.78.0**, the latest version without issues for Bootstrap (as of now).
- **Silence the warnings** if your build system allows it.
- **Ignore them** for now—these are just warnings, and the deprecated features are still functional.
- **Check out these issues** for workarounds or discussion:
  - https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/40621  
  - https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/40849  
  - https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/40962  

It's not released yet, but our documentation, from v5.3.6, will temporarily include solutions for tools like Parcel, Webpack, and Vite in our ['next' documentation](https://twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app), or directly in our Bootstrap examples:
- [Parcel starter](https://github.com/twbs/examples/tree/main/parcel)
- [Vite starter](https://github.com/twbs/examples/tree/main/vite)
- [Vue starter](https://github.com/twbs/examples/tree/main/vue)
- [Webpack starter](https://github.com/twbs/examples/tree/main/webpack)

---

## 🧨 Sass 1.79.x Introduced a Breaking Change

Starting with **Sass 1.79.x**, things get trickier: the `red()`, `green()`, and `blue()` functions were deprecated in favor of `color.channel()`. Unfortunately:

- `color.channel()` isn’t supported by Node Sass
- There’s no workaround for this on our side

This means: to fix these and other Sass deprecation issues coming from 1.80+, **we have to drop Node Sass support**.

---

## 🪄 Step 1: Moving Docs from Hugo to Astro

Our documentation currently uses **Hugo**, which depends by default on Node Sass. That’s a blocker.

We’re migrating to **Astro**, which fully supports Dart Sass (and for other reasons as well):

🔧 PR in progress: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/41251

This will be released in **v5.3.6** (or another v5.3.x), a patch release, since it doesn't impact how Bootstrap works—just the way we build and ship the docs.

**This is our primary focus at the time of writing.**

Once that’s done, we'll fix bugs quickly in follow-ups like **v5.3.7**, **v5.3.8**, etc. Community help testing this will be hugely appreciated! 🙏 

---

## ✨ v5.4.0: Final Feature Release

After the docs work wraps up, we’ll ship **v5.4.0**, the **last Bootstrap 5 release with new features**.

Many of the PRs for this have already been reviewed and are ready to merge. We still need to go through a triage phase for the [v5.4.0 GitHub project](https://github.com/orgs/twbs/projects/12), but the goal is to keep that process lightweight and not spend too much time on it.

---

## 🔥 v5.5.0: Dropping Node Sass & Switching to Sass Modules

> [!WARNING]  
> **Update from @mdo on 4/13/25:** We’d really love to make getting on Sass modules into a v5.x release, but it'll all depend on the amount of changes necessary. If we can't do it, this will move to a v6 release.

After v5.4, we’ll ship **v5.5.0**, which will:

- Drop Node Sass support completely  
- Refactor our Sass code to use the **Sass module system** (`@use`, `@forward`)  
- Clean up all remaining deprecation warnings  

This will be a **breaking change for Sass users**, but it’s necessary for long-term compatibility.

Sass users will need to:

- Use `@use` instead of `@import`  
- Explicitly load modules like `@use "sass:color"`, `@use "sass:math"`
- And maybe other stuff not yet identifeid

That’s a big change that needs to happen really soon, but we believe it’s manageable with clear communication.

> **Why not call this v6?** Because it doesn’t change how Bootstrap is *used*, only how it’s *compiled*. We think v6 should represent a bigger conceptual shift. Like a new epoch.

---

## 🔭 Looking Ahead: Bootstrap v6

We have started the [development of Bootstrap v6](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/41236), and we want it to be more than just a technical update—it should reflect a new **philosophy or vision** for Bootstrap.

That’s why:

- Dropping Node Sass and switching to modules is **not** v6 material on its own  
- v6 will introduce broader changes, possibly even new paradigms  
- We’re studying the _Epoch Semantic Versioning_, where major versions could be a combination between new epochs that mark conceptual shifts (what we used to call v5, v6, etc.), and breaking changes like regular Semver. An implementation could be something as described as in the [Epoch Semantic Versioning - by Anthony Fu](https://antfu.me/posts/epoch-semver) 🤷 

---

## TL;DR – Bootstrap Release Roadmap

| Version      | What's in it?                                                                      |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **v5.3.4 / v5.3.5** | Fixed Sass deprecation warnings for Sass 1.77.7                            |
| **v5.3.6**    | Migrates docs from Hugo to Astro (drops Node Sass usage in docs)            |
| **v5.3.7+**   | (Optional) Bug fixes for new docs setup                                                        |
| **v5.4.0**    | Final v5 release with new features                                                 |
| **v5.5.0**    | Breaking: drops Node Sass, switches to Sass modules, resolves all warnings       |
| **v6.x**      | Will be a conceptual reset, not just technical updates; a new epoch            |

---

title: Am I just old fashion who don't like learning new things or just more complex than it needs to be?
body:
Hi all

First up, while I might be new here and new to actility trying to use bootstrap, I have heard of this project for some years. 

With my experience with working with css, i've always just started writing out css in a pure .css file, no fancy editing software needed, notepad would do too. I worked with css in wordpress and PHPBB 3.0.x and never needed anything more than a text editor to edit those files. While I do have phpstorm, it was very simple to work with.

While reading up on how to go about setting custom colours etc, I started reading into terms like Sass and so on. My question is, why?

Maybe i'm just so used to the days where PHPBB had their css split up into a bunch of css files with one master one including the content of the other css files. So I want to change the colour of something? Just pop open the required css file, edit, save and done. No need to much around with Sass or any of that stuff.

Am I really overthinking stuff or am I just one of those who think that new stuff isn't walways better than doing things the old fashion way?

##### PS, W3School have been helpful on how to use this framework

title: Add Extra Bootstrap Ratios for All Breakpoints
body:
Hello Bootstrap Team,

I propose adding support for extra aspect ratios across all breakpoints, as outlined in this Gist: https://gist.github.com/uzielweb/974a7ddc678fd124bce8b7c69ebb886e

This feature would enhance flexibility for responsive designs by allowing developers to define additional aspect ratios (e.g., for images, videos, or containers) that adapt to Bootstrap's breakpoint system. The Gist includes a suggested implementation with utility classes like .ratio-{breakpoint}-{ratio} (e.g., .ratio-md-16x9, .ratio-lg-4x3) to maintain consistency with Bootstrap's naming conventions.

Proposed Benefits:

- Improved control over aspect ratios in responsive layouts.
- Seamless integration with Bootstrap's existing grid and utility systems.
- Enhanced developer experience for media-heavy or custom layouts.

Implementation Notes:

- The Gist provides a starting point with CSS custom properties and media queries.
- Ensure compatibility with existing .ratio classes and avoid breaking changes.
- Consider adding Sass mixins for easier customization in source builds.

Please review the Gist and let me know if further refinements or test cases are needed. Happy to contribute to the discussion or code!

Thank you, in advance.

title: Bootstrap Collapse JS API with Angular
body:
Alright, I'm not filing this under bugs, but I'd really like someone who knows what their doing to tell me why this is not a bug. And I'm going to do something dastardly and include a full chatgpt chat on the subject, including hallucinations, for you to peruse. The Tl;DR of that is that I am using bootstrap v5.3 with angular v19 and am generally using functionality that takes advantage of data-bs-toggle (Dropdowns, Collapses) all over the site. In one area I want to use the bootstrap JS API to show (not toggle) a particular collapse. Integrating the code to do that into my angular component any which way me or the AI can think of results in either it simply not working, or it **breaks all data-bs-toggle functionality all over the page**! I would love someone to tell me how stupid I am. Preferably in a constructive fix the problem way rather than a non-constructive that's what you get for using angular way. Anyway, feel free to have fun with this one... --- **Edited by maintainer**: hidden the PDF link for security reasons. If OP can copy and paste the content here 🙏 --- Here is the content pasted from the previous chatgpt pdf transcript as suggested by the maintainer, but due to formatting it is basically unreadable. :-( Angular Bootstrap Collapse Toggle Using angular v19 and bootstrap v5.3, I would like to activate a bootstrap collapse by calling a function in my angular component. How can I do this? To programmatically toggle a Bootstrap 5.3 Collapse from an Angular v19 component, you can use Bootstrap’s JavaScript API ( Collapse class from bootstrap ) in combination with @ViewChild or document.querySelector . Here's a step-by-step example: ✅ 1. Install Bootstrap (if not already done) Then include it in angular.json or your styles/scripts config. ✅ 2. Import Bootstrap Collapse JS (important!) Angular doesn't automatically include Bootstrap JS, so you must do it manually. Either: Include it in angular.json : OR Dynamically import in your component: bash npm install bootstrap json "scripts": ["./node_modules/bootstrap/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"] ts import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; 1/19 ✅ 3. Template: Add the collapsible content ✅ 4. Component: Control the Collapse html
This is collapsible content.
ts // template.component.ts import { Component, ElementRef, ViewChild } from '@angular/core'; import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; @Component({ selector: 'app-template', templateUrl: './template.component.html', }) export class TemplateComponent { @ViewChild('collapseDiv', { static: true }) collapseDiv!: ElementRef; private bsCollapse!: Collapse; ngOnInit() { this.bsCollapse = new Collapse(this.collapseDiv.nativeElement, { toggle: false, // Important: don’t toggle immediately }); } toggleCollapse() { this.bsCollapse.toggle(); 2/19 ✅ Notes toggle: false ensures the collapse is not toggled immediately on init. You can also use .show() and .hide() methods instead of .toggle() . Would you like an example using signals or reactive state to drive the collapse? } } This works but it prevents my from using the data-toggle method of toggling the collapse as well. Possibly because the new Collapse() call in ngOnInit() reinitializing the collapse the data-toggle is set to use. I would like to be able to use the data-toggle and the function in my component to toggle the collapse. Is this possible? You're absolutely right — when you instantiate a Bootstrap Collapse instance manually with new Collapse(...) , it overrides or duplicates the behavior that the data-bstoggle="collapse" mechanism sets up automatically via Bootstrap’s internal initialization. This leads to conflicts where toggling via a button using data-bs-toggle no longer works. ✅ Goal Recap You want to: Toggle the collapse using both: Angular function ( toggleCollapse() ) HTML button with data-bs-toggle="collapse" ✅ Solution Instead of calling new Collapse(...) manually (which reinitializes it), you can query the existing instance initialized by Bootstrap’s data-bs-toggle mechanism and call its 3/19 methods. Bootstrap 5.3 exposes a way to get the existing instance via: So your Angular code can safely retrieve (but not reinitialize) the component. ✅ Updated Example HTML Component ts Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(element) html
Collapsible content.
4/19 ✅ Why This Works The HTML data-bs-toggle="collapse" initializes the collapse once the page loads. Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...) allows you to safely access that instance, ensuring you're not creating a duplicate or conflicting one. You can now control the collapse both from HTML and from your component without interference. Would you like this tied to a reactive Angular signal as well? ts import { Component, ElementRef, ViewChild, AfterViewInit } from '@angular/core'; import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; @Component({ selector: 'app-bootstrap-collapse', templateUrl: './bootstrap-collapse.component.html' }) export class BootstrapCollapseComponent implements AfterViewInit { @ViewChild('collapseDiv') collapseDiv!: ElementRef; private collapseInstance!: Collapse; ngAfterViewInit() { // Don't re-initialize — just retrieve the one created by data-bs-toggle this.collapseInstance = Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(this.collapseDiv.nativeElement); } toggleCollapse() { this.collapseInstance.toggle(); } } Hmm. This is allowing the data-bs-toggle to open the collapse, but it is not closing the collapse when clicked a 5/19 second time. What is going on? Excellent observation — and you're hitting a subtle but known behavior quirk with Bootstrap’s Collapse + Angular + manual control. ❗ What's happening data-bs-toggle="collapse" sets up event handlers via Bootstrap's internal event system (using native JavaScript, not Angular). If you use Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...) before Bootstrap’s internal initialization (via data-bs-toggle ) happens — e.g., during ngAfterViewInit — you override the internal toggler behavior, or only partially hook into it. As a result, clicking the button opens the collapse, but subsequent clicks do nothing, because the internal toggle state and event handlers are out of sync. ✅ Solution Instead of calling Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() too early, wait until the DOM and Bootstrap's automatic behavior have finished initializing. You can delay the manual access using setTimeout , or better, defer it until after user interaction, like so: ✅ Option 1: Lazy-access the instance only when needed ts toggleCollapse() { const collapseElement = this.collapseDiv.nativeElement; const instance = Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(collapseElement); instance.toggle(); } 6/19 ✅ Option 2: Use a setTimeout to let Bootstrap initialize first (not ideal, but works) ⚠️ But this is a hack. Prefer Option 1. ✅ Bonus Tip: Ensure Bootstrap initializes collapse properly Bootstrap initializes elements marked with data-bs-toggle="collapse" only if the document has fully loaded, or if you explicitly call: You can put this in a DOMContentLoaded listener or call it in a shared Bootstrap-init script if needed. This ensures Bootstrap has already initialized the collapse, and you simply reuse the instance at the moment you need it. ts ngAfterViewInit() { setTimeout(() => { this.collapseInstance = Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(this.collapseDiv.nativeElement); }); } ts import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; // Manually initialize everything (only needed if Bootstrap isn't auto-initing) document.querySelectorAll('.collapse').forEach((el) => { Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(el); }); 7/19 ✅ TL;DR Use this pattern in Angular for coexistence with data-bs-toggle : And let Bootstrap own the initialization lifecycle. Would you like this wrapped into a reusable Angular directive or service? ts toggleCollapse() { const instance = Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(this.collapseDiv.nativeElement); instance.toggle(); } Implementing your suggested solution from your TL;DR has led to an interesting discovery. If I use any of the bootstrap Collapse creation methods, 'new Collapse()', 'Collapse.getOrCreateInstance()', or 'Collapse.getInstance()', anywhere on my page it breaks all data-bs-toggle functionality everywhere on the page, even in unrelated components and other bootstrap elements. For instance, it breaks the bootstrap Dropdown functionality elsewhere in the page because that functionality also uses data-bscollapse. Do you know what is causing this? Yes — and you've uncovered a critical but under-documented Bootstrap 5.3 behavior when using its JavaScript APIs manually inside Angular apps. ❗ What’s actually going on Bootstrap v5.3 uses its own data API to automatically initialize components like Collapse, Dropdown, Modal, etc., when the page loads. This is typically done via: js import 'bootstrap'; // or bootstrap.bundle.min.js 8/19 This internally triggers logic that binds event listeners and manages toggler-target relationships ( data-bs-toggle , data-bs-target , etc.). However: This leads to: Buttons with data-bs-toggle="dropdown" or data-bs-toggle="collapse" breaking. Dropdowns and collapses becoming non-functional. Toggle events no longer working across the page. ✅ Why this happens Bootstrap's Data API and manual JS API are not fully isolated. When you manually instantiate a Collapse , you: Overwrite data-bs-toggle behavior by forcibly binding to the element. Cause Bootstrap to skip or fail to attach internal delegated event listeners, especially if Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() is called too early. Potentially register conflicting event handlers. This side-effect cascades across the page, affecting all Bootstrap components that rely on shared initialization or global event delegation. ✅ Best Practices for Angular + Bootstrap 1. Avoid calling Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() or new Collapse() unless absolutely necessary. You don’t need it if you're using data-bs-toggle . 2. Let Bootstrap handle everything via data-bs-toggle if you can. Don't manually interact with the instance. ❗ When you manually create or retrieve any Collapse instance via new Collapse() or Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() before the data API has fully initialized, it shortcircuits Bootstrap’s global auto-initialization lifecycle. 9/19 3. If you need to control a collapse programmatically, manually dispatch a click event to the toggle element instead of calling Collapse methods directly. ✅ Alternative (safe) workaround: Trigger via native click Instead of doing: Do: This: Triggers Bootstrap's own toggle system Doesn't interfere with global event handling Keeps data-bs-toggle functional across the page ✅ Summary Action Effect Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() ✅ Works, but can silently break data-bs-toggle new Collapse() ⚠️ Re-initializes, overrides, and may break dropdowns, modals, and collapses Dispatching click to [data-bs-toggle] ✅ Safe, Bootstrap-friendly, and non-intrusive Letting data-bs-toggle handle everything ✅ Best default behavior in Angular apps ts Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...).toggle(); // risky ts document.querySelector('[data-bs-target="#collapseExample"]')?.dispatchEvent(new Event('click')); 10/19 Would you like a small Angular directive that safely toggles a collapse via dispatched click, while keeping compatibility intact? Something is still not making sense to me. The data-bstoggle functionality is breaking even if my call to Collapse.getOrCreateInstance() is inside a click handler and the element attached to that handler has not been clicked. I.e. it seems that just having that call somewhere in the code, even if it has not actually been called, triggers the issue. In this case, how can simply having the Collapse function in an uncalled function break the data-bshandling? You're absolutely right to be puzzled — because this behavior is unexpected and subtle. But there is an explanation that makes sense once you understand what Bootstrap's JavaScript does internally — and it’s related not to whether you call Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...) , but to how the module is imported and bundled in Angular's build system. 🔍 What's Actually Happening Even if your Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...) call is inside an uncalled function, just importing the Collapse class from Bootstrap like this: …can break data-bs-toggle behaviors across the page. ❗Why? Because just importing Collapse or any other component from Bootstrap's ESM modules disables its auto-initialization via data attributes. ts import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; Bootstrap’s data API initialization ( data-bs-toggle , etc.) is only active when you use the full UMD bundle, which includes the automatic behavior for toggling components based on attributes. 11/19 When you use: …you're using tree-shakable, module-scoped Bootstrap, which: Does not globally register event handlers for data-bs-toggle . Assumes you will manage all initialization manually via new Collapse(...) . This means: ✅ The Fix You need to import the Bootstrap UMD bundle, which auto-registers the data-bs-toggle behavior globally. ✅ In angular.json Important: Once you're using this approach, do not also import named ESM modules like import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap' , or you’ll cause conflicts. ✅ Option: Keep Using ESM, But Manually Init If you prefer the import { Collapse } style (e.g. for smaller bundle size), you must manually initialize all collapses, dropdowns, etc., like this: ts import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap'; ✔ You get fine-grained control over components ❌ You lose all data-bs-toggle functionality unless you manually initialize every component json "scripts": ["node_modules/bootstrap/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"] This bundle includes Popper.js and registers the global listeners for collapse, dropdown, modal, tooltip, etc. 12/19 Place that in your root component or a bootstrap-init service. ✅ Summary Scenario Result ✅ Only use UMD bootstrap.bundle.min.js in angular.json data-bs-toggle works everywhere automatically ❌ Use import { Collapse } from bootstrap (ESM) data-bs-toggle silently breaks unless you manually init ❌ Mix UMD and ESM Conflicts and breakage (do not mix) ✅ Recommendation for Angular apps: ✅ Use the UMD script in angular.json if you rely on data-bs-toggle . 🚫 Avoid importing individual Bootstrap modules like Collapse unless you want to manually manage every instance. ts import { Collapse, Dropdown } from 'bootstrap'; document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="collapse"]').forEach(el => { new Collapse(el); }); document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="dropdown"]').forEach(el => { new Dropdown(el); }); 13/19 Would you like a complete example of an Angular Bootstrap setup that preserves full datatoggle functionality while supporting programmatic control? Am I right that using the UMD in angular.json will not allow me to also call Collapse functions in my Angular Components? Yes, you're mostly correct — using the UMD version via angular.json does not make Bootstrap's Collapse class directly importable into your Angular components via ESM import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap' . Here's the full picture: ✅ What Happens When You Use UMD via angular.json When you add this: Angular will: Load the UMD bundle globally at runtime Bootstrap will attach its functionality (like data-bs-toggle ) to the window object ✅ data-bs-toggle , dropdowns, modals, collapse — all work out of the box ❌ You cannot import { Collapse } from 'bootstrap' in your TypeScript code (it’s not available via module import) 🧠 Why You Can’t Use Collapse in Components (with UMD) The UMD bundle is a runtime global script, not a module. So Collapse is not available to ES module imports. However... json "scripts": ["node_modules/bootstrap/dist/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js"] 14/19 ✅ Workaround: Access Collapse from Global Scope If you still want to programmatically control collapse while using the UMD approach, you can grab the Collapse class off the global window object: 👇 Example: This works because Bootstrap 5.2+ (when using the UMD bundle) exposes all components globally via window.bootstrap . ✅ Summary Use Case UMD in angular.json ESM import { Collapse } data-bs-toggle auto behavior ✅ Yes ❌ No Programmatic use in TS Via window.bootstrap.Collapse ✅ Yes Tree-shaking & minimal bundle ❌ No (includes all components) ✅ Yes Can mix UMD + ESM safely ❌ No ❌ No ✅ Recommendation ts declare const window: any; toggleCollapse() { const el = document.getElementById('collapseExample'); const Collapse = window.bootstrap.Collapse; // Access Collapse from global if (Collapse && el) { const instance = Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(el); instance.toggle(); } } 15/19 If you rely on data-bs-toggle and want to occasionally control a collapse programmatically: ✅ Use the UMD bundle in angular.json ✅ Access Collapse via window.bootstrap.Collapse in your Angular code Would you like me to generate a minimal reusable Angular service or directive that wraps this behavior cleanly? Although the IDE does not show any error, compiling the angular project produces the following error: [ERROR] TS2339: Property 'bootstrap' does not exist on type 'Window & typeof globalThis'. [plugin angularcompiler] src/app/shared/post/post.component.ts:117:28: 117 │ const Collapse = window.bootstrap.Collapse; ╵ ~~~~~~~~~ Thanks — you're encountering that error because TypeScript doesn't know that window.bootstrap exists when you're using the UMD Bootstrap bundle (since it's injected globally at runtime, not typed). ✅ Solution: Extend the global Window interface to declare bootstrap Here’s the correct and type-safe way to handle this in an Angular project: ✅ Step 1: Add the global typing (e.g., in your post.component.ts or a separate types.d.ts file) ts declare global { interface Window { bootstrap: { Collapse: any; // You can add Dropdown, Modal, etc. here if needed }; 16/19 This tells TypeScript: “Yes, I know window.bootstrap will be there at runtime.” You can place this at the top of your component file or ideally in a shared typings file like src/types/bootstrap.d.ts . ✅ Step 2: Use it in your component ✅ Optional: If you want full types You can also install Bootstrap’s types (though they’re incomplete): And reference them, but they mostly cover utility types and won’t cover the global bootstrap object — hence the need for manual declaration. Let me know if you want a global wrapper service that avoids window altogether. } } ts toggleCollapse() { const el = document.getElementById('collapseExample'); if (el) { const collapseInstance = window.bootstrap.Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(el); collapseInstance.toggle(); } } ✅ Now TypeScript won’t complain during compilation, and the runtime behavior will work as expected with UMD-loaded Bootstrap. bash npm install --save-dev @types/bootstrap 17/19 If I put that in a separate types.d.ts file, how do I import it so that it is applied to in my post.component.ts file? Great question — and here's the good news: ✅ Step-by-step to make it work: 1. Create the file Place it in your src/ folder (or a subfolder like src/types/ ), for example: 2. Add this to the file 3. Ensure it's included in TypeScript's compilation As long as the file is under your tsconfig.json 's "include" path (usually "src/**/*" ), it will be picked up automatically. ✅ You don’t need to explicitly import types.d.ts anywhere as long as it's in your tsconfig 's scope (typically under src/ ). TypeScript will automatically include it globally during compilation. bash src/types/bootstrap.d.ts ts // src/types/bootstrap.d.ts export {}; // Ensures this file is treated as a module declare global { interface Window { bootstrap: { Collapse: any; Dropdown?: any; // Optional: add others as needed }; } } 18/19 Here's what it should look like by default: ✅ No need to import it manually in post.component.ts . You can now safely use: with full TypeScript awareness. Let me know if you'd like to create a CollapseService to avoid using window directly in your components. json { "compilerOptions": { ... }, "include": ["src/**/*"] } ts window.bootstrap.Collapse.getOrCreateInstance(...) 19/19

title: reCAPTCHA 401 Unauthorized Error in Chrome Console
body:
I’m integrating Google reCAPTCHA v2 Checkbox into my web application to verify form submissions, but I’m encountering a 401 Unauthorized error in the Chrome Developer Console. The form submission (used to send emails) works, but the reCAPTCHA request fails, and I need help resolving this issue.

Issue Description

When submitting a form with reCAPTCHA, the following error appears in the Chrome Developer Console:

POST https://www.google.com/recaptcha/api2/pat?k=6LeguEMrAAAAAOvFCBJnPZv1hl0zVJYxg9jPiTI5 401 (Unauthorized)

This suggests the reCAPTCHA API request is unauthorized. I’ve verified that:

The reCAPTCHA site key is correct and matches the one in the Google Cloud Console.

The domain (localhost for testing) is listed in the reCAPTCHA settings.

The secret key is used for backend verification.

Despite the error, the form submission and email functionality work, indicating that reCAPTCHA verification might not be properly enforced.

Environment

reCAPTCHA Version: v2 Checkbox

Environment: Local development (localhost)

Browser: Chrome (latest version)

Backend: Node.js with Express

Error Details: The POST request to /recaptcha/api2/pat fails with a 401 status. No additional response details are visible in the Network tab.

Questions

What could cause a 401 Unauthorized error for a reCAPTCHA API request?

Are there specific settings in the Google Cloud Console or reCAPTCHA configuration I should check?

How can I debug this issue to ensure reCAPTCHA verifies correctly?

What I’ve Tried

Confirmed the site key and secret key are correct in the Google Cloud Console.

Added localhost to the allowed domains in reCAPTCHA settings.

Tested in an incognito window to rule out browser extensions blocking the request.

Verified that the reCAPTCHA script loads correctly in the browser.

I’d appreciate any insights or suggestions on resolving this error. Let me know if specific configuration details would help!

Additional Information: Here are the error screenshots for reference: https://ibb.co/fVC7sw4W https://ibb.co/Q7qdzzmF

Thanks, Ary

author: pardeyke

title: I built a Bootstrap wrapper with more components, a simple color system, and modern animations 🎉
body:
Hey everyone 👋

Like many of you, I’ve been hacking Bootstrap for years — wiring tooltips manually, fighting the color system, swapping out clunky transitions.

So I built [Bravo](https://bravo.appitude.io/): a thin enhancement layer on top of Bootstrap that bakes in the fixes I always wished existed.

**A few highlights:**
- 🎨 Colors that actually scale – every color (including custom ones) expands into a full stepped palette (25 → 975), all as CSS vars + utilities.
- ⚡ Automatic wiring – the Dynamic Observer auto-inits tooltips, dropdowns, popovers, and button loaders. No more new Tooltip(...) sprinkled everywhere.
- 📦 Components you wish existed – programmatic modals, button loaders, interactive tooltips, hover-trigger dropdowns.
- 📱 Layouts that fit today – responsive container gutters, safe-area support, and a cleaner .column-* alias.
- ✨ Animations that feel modern – smoother transitions that don’t feel stuck in 2015.

It’s still Bootstrap under the hood — Bravo just trims some cruft, tweaks defaults, and adds quality-of-life features.


**👉 I’d love feedback from the community:**
- Do these fill the same gaps you usually patch yourself?
- Anything else you’ve always wanted Bootstrap to handle?

Repo: [github.com/appitudeio/bravo](https://github.com/appitudeio/bravo)

Docs: [bravo.appitude.io](https://bravo.appitude.io/)

Thanks for reading — hope it’s useful! 🚀

title: Unwanted scrolling with checkbox toggle buttons
body: This is a very strange thing that happens to me on a Sveltekit project I'm working on. The issue has 2 symptoms in my case: 1. A second scrollbar appears. 2. Clicking the checkbox toggle button scrolls this second scrollbar. Reproduction? Sorry, I have none, but I can show you screenshots: The application layout is that of a PWA, where there's a top navbar (circle 1 in the screenshot), a footer (not in the screenshot), and "the rest" area which is the scrollable area. This means that the application always show the navbar and the footer. As seen, there are 2 scrollbars: Circle 2 is the intended scrollbar, which starts right after the navbar (and ends before the footer), but the second one is for the entire HTML document (in devtools, the HTML element is signaled as scrollable). This happens by simply adding one of the button variants to the markup. ## The Root Cause The `.btn-check` class sets the input's `position` attribute to `absolute`, and this causes the input box to be positioned "outside". Outside what? Don't know. I think it is positioned at the end of the contents between the navbar and the footer. In other words, I think would not be experimenting this problem if the content between navbar and footer was small in height. Anyway, because the browser places the invisible input element in this position, this double scrollbar situation arises. The fix: Give it a parent with class `.position-relative`. Because my guess is that this only happens to me (unlucky me) I suppose it doesn't warrant a fix, but I wanted to document it for anyone seeing this.

title: BS5 tooltips got mixed in with BS4 tooltips and now are bugged
body:
I have a web app that we recently upgraded from bootstrap 4 to bootstrap 5.3.8. For the past month or so, I've been going through and fixing broken layouts/colors/etc. due to this upgrade. I thought it would be cool to add BS5 tooltips on some buttons, so I made a branch and merged it with dev for new BS5 tooltips.

However, I did not realize the scope of which I was initializing the BS5 tooltips. The new ones seemed to have taken over our old, existing BS4 tooltips that were still using jquery. The main issue I ran into was that tooltips for fields with validation no longer updated and persisted even after the field passed validation i.e. it still said "[fieldName] is required" with a new BS5 tooltip even after I fill in the field name, whereas the old BS4 jquery tooltips did not do that before.

So we reverted dev back to a previous commit before I introduced BS5 tooltips, but the same issue persists. There's no code that initializes BS5 tooltips in this reverted version. I tried removing node_modules and then doing npm install, but that did not fix it. We are also using angular and I read somewhere that I may need to clear angular's cache.

I'm just wondering if anyone else has ran into this issue and/or has any possible solutions? I also understand that replacing our jquery with the new way to initialize/dispose/update tooltips would maybe work, but I'm wondering if there's a simpler solution than that.

title: Tooltips and Accessibility
body:
I wanted to follow up on issue https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/39945 and my comment there. It seems to me that setting `title=""` for an element with tooltip is intended to prevent the BS tooltip from colliding with the browser’s default tooltip for that same element. However, that `""` makes the title text unavailable for ATs to announce the element. A solution to that seems to be the use of the [aria-description](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Attributes/aria-description) attribute, but that’s not documented for [v4.6](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/4.6/components/tooltips/) or [v5.1](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/components/tooltips/). For testing purposes with ATs I used this test file: ```html Test Links

A link: Google

A link with Tooltip: Google

A link with Tooltip und ARIA 1: Google

A link with Tooltip und ARIA 2: Google

A link with Tooltip und ARIA 3: Google

A link with Tooltip und ARIA 4: Google

Link to the Google search engine

``` Interestingly, Aria 1 and 3 are announced by an AT (I think it’s JAWS) and 2 and 4 are not (maybe because `#desc` is isn’t displayed). Now I’m curious about the community’s suggestions… 🤓 **Addendum** see also [ARIA: tooltip role](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/tooltip_role) on MDN.
author: jenstroeger

title: How can I prevent inactive Bootstrap buttons from showing as active when they are in focus on iOS?
body:
I'm using buttons and transparent pills in Bootstrap for filters on a page. This works great everywhere except iOS, where if a user taps on a button to make it inactive, the button will be inactive but will still appear to be active until the user taps somewhere else. For clarity, I'm talking about the button or pill background being filled, not the focus outline, which is not bothersome. AI has suggested that I use JavaScript `blur()` to force rpainting of the button, but that has its own issue of needing to keep track of each button state in JavaScript rather than CSS so that buttons that are toggled as active don't get repainted as inactive. I've tried checking for the `active` class on click, and only calling `blur` if the button no longer has the `active` class, but that check didn't work, likely because of a race condition between removing the `active` class, and checking for the class.

I have also tried this CSS without success.

```css
button, .btn {
    -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent !important; /* Prevent iOS highlight */
    touch-action: manipulation !important; /* Prevent unintended active effects */
}
```

Is there a better solution?

title: Unified color declaring method or a color pallete
body:
No offence guys :)

Why you keep releasing CSS using so many duplicated color declaring methods?

`  --bs-body-color: #212529;
  --bs-body-color-rgb: 33, 37, 41;`

You can easily replace all these using this and preserve all functionality as it is, this is a super quick fix.

`color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--bs-link-color) calc(var(--bs-link-opacity, 1) * 100%), transparent);`

Even better would be a unified color + alpha or something

`--bs-body-color: #212529FF;`

Or, nice to have a true, separate pallete.css having all color definitions.
Now, few of them ar in :roo[] other hard coded all over file.

Just a idea :)

title: Unclear documentation on the dispose method: calling it after hide
body:
In [JavaScript, dispose method](https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.3/getting-started/javascript/#dispose-method), it mentions that there is a problem calling `dispose` after the component is hidden, but it doesn't explain much.

> While it may seem correct to use the `dispose` method immediately after `hide()`, it will lead to incorrect results. Here’s an example of the problem use:

```js
const myModal = document.querySelector('#myModal')
myModal.hide() // it is asynchronous

myModal.addEventListener('shown.bs.hidden', event => {
  myModal.dispose()
})
```

It's listening for the end of the transitions, so it looks correct to me. What exactly is the problem there?

I've found [this possibly related issue](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/41473) and [this one](https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/issues/37265), but that's all.
