author: Thiti517

title: Pagination component does not match the input/button components
body: I'd like to suggest improving the `Pagination` component to match the `Input` / `Button` components height In the past, Bootstrap 4 & 5, matched the `Pagination` component's height to `Input` and `Button` components, allowing us to add in-line components without messing with the height. Currently, both `input` and `button` components apply a `min-height` on them. While the `pagination` component respects the `font-size`, `line-height`, and `padding`s, it does not apply the `min-height`. An example of such case is a "Go to" input to assist the `Pagination` component, which is extremely common for tables. This causes the following issue when used with inline `inputs` and `buttons` (look at the red lines & spacing on the `pagination` component): The fix would be to make the `pagination` match the the `input` and `button` height, simiarly to how Bootstrap 4 & Boostrap 5 worked. For reference, there's a super well documented article on "Manual input pagination" forms at https://app.uxcel.com/courses/ui-components-best-practices/anatomy-216#manual-input-7118 which shows how a manual input for pagination would work alongside pagination buttons. I've opened a PR for this: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42137 so you can check it out.
author: pricop

title: Input & button height adjustments
body: I've looked over the new v6 docs, and the `-sm` input's height is too big in my opinion. While other `-sm` components, such as `pagination` are around `2rem` (`32px`), inputs and buttons have been increased to `2.25rem` (`36px`). Generally, for `sm` variants of `input` and `button` components, frameworks such as Bootstrap 4, Boostrap 5, Tailwind, Catalyst, and others, default to `32px` for `sm` variants. While I understand that Bootstrap 6 can be opinionated, I believe this is overexaggerated for a `sm` variant. The `sm` variants generally are for dense UIs where space is of the essence.bad. I'd like to propose changing: `xs`: `24px` `sm`: `36px` (`12px` gap compared to `xs` variant) `md`: `40px`, (`4px` gap compared to `sm` variant) `lg`: `48px` (`8px` gap compared to `md` variant) to the following values: `xs`: `24px` `sm`: `32px` (`8px` gap compared to `xs`) `md`: `38px`, (`6px` gap compared to `sm`) `lg`: `44px` (`6px` gap compared to `md`) Please note how the gap between variants is more uniform now, from `12 → 4 → 8` to `8 → 6 → 6`. Visual comparison: --- Real-world case examples: Here's a screenshot with `-sm` `input` and `button` at `2rem` height (`32px`), which has very good proportions. Now, here's the same dashboard with `sm` input and buttons in the header, which currently defaults to `2.25rem` (`36px`). You can probably observe that it looks out of proportions. --- Another use case is for `sm` variants for `input` and `button` components is to be used alongside `pagination` component, which currently sits at around 2rem (`31px` to be more precise, but that's due to a bug, I've submmitted a PR for that as well at https://github.com/orgs/twbs/discussions/42136) So in my opinion, it would be much better to make the `sm` variants have a height of `2rem` (`32px`), which in turn will also make it look nice when used in table headers, as well as table footers, and other denser UIs, see: --- I've opened a PR at: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42139 for you to review. Thank you.

author: PieterCooreman

title: Link & Pill shaped nav items height
body: Currently, the `nav-link` and `nav-pills` items sit at `36px` in height. This is due to having no border around them. By contrast, `nav-tabs`, and `nav-underline`, have a height of `38px` (2.25rem), this is because `nav-tabs` have a `1px` border around them, while `nav-underline` have 2px underneath each element. My proposed change is to increase the height of `nav-link` and `nav-pills` to 38px, by adding an invisible border to match the height of `nav-tabs` and `nav-underline` counterparts. Additionally, it's super common to use an in-page `nav` alongside a search `input` component, or an additional `button` component (that's outside the scope of the nav itself), for example: **Before** **After** This will make all `nav` variants (`link`, `pill`, `tab`, `underline`) cohesive, having the same height across the board, regardless of each version is used. --- I've done a PR with the change for you guys to have a look at: https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42146
author: pricop

title: Building Components On The Fly
body:
I have two libraries that dynamically build Bootstrap components on the fly that I thought worth sharing:

1. Form Builder: https://github.com/dday9/Bootstrap-Form-Builder
2. Modal Extensions: https://github.com/dday9/bootstrap-modal-extension

Both libraries do similar things in that they take a JavaScript object and convert it to a Bootstrap component.

The form builder library builds forms and supports pretty much every input aside from buttons and images. It also supports:

- Laying out the controls via the Bootstrap grid system
- Dynamically creating `<datalist>` elements
- Converting checkbox inputs into switches

The modal extensions library builds modals with optional headers/footers. It also has these prebuilt modals:

- alert
- confirm
- prompt

The next library I plan on building is a toast library.

I've been using Bootstrap since version 3, and it has made me a lot of money during my career. I am very grateful to the Bootstrap team for all that they have done in providing such a wonderful CSS framework.

PS - Thank God for LLMs. ChatGPT wrote the technical documentation for those libraries for me. If you can't tell from this discussion, I don't spellin an grammur good, so having it write out the docs for me was incredibly helpful.
author: dday9

title: Proper floating labels on selects have been possible for a while now; how do we want to target the placeholder option?
body:
With the wide adoption of the `:has` selector, making floating labels on select inputs behave like they do on text inputs is now possible ([example](https://jsfiddle.net/aL75feku/)).

I'm ready to make a pull request to update the SCSS/docs for this, but I'm not sure how the community would prefer the targeting of the placeholder option to be handled.

My personal preference is to simply target the empty option - `option:empty` - since it requires the least markup and would likely have the widest drop-in compatibility with various form frameworks, but others might prefer to avoid edge cases and require a class to be added to the option - `option.placeholder`.
author: ajdinmore

title: Width / Height utility percentage value names
body:
### **LATER EDIT**

I'd like to suggest changing the percentage based utility names from:

```
25: 25%,
50: 50%,
75: 75%,
100: 100%,
```

to:
```
25p: 25%,
50p: 50%,
75p: 75%,
100p: 100%,
```

or:
```
25\/100: 25%,
50\/100: 50%,
75\/100: 75%,
100\/100: 100%,
```

So rather than writing `w-50`, you'd be writing `w-50p` or `w-50/100`.

The scope of this change is the removal of class names from `$sizes` map conflicting with the percentage class names from the utilities map. Additionally, it would open up the possibility of using `$sizes` with smaller increments, such as `.25rem` (similar to Tailwind), without quickly overlapping with the percentage value classes.

This also means that people can just chip-in additional percentage based values, such as `w-10/100` by setting `10\/100`: 10%` for `10%` without interfering with the `w-10` which is set to `10rem`, and also just be consistent across multiple projects that may be using Bootstrap.

This would apply to `w`, `h`, `min-w`, `max-w`, `min-viewport-width`, `min-viewport-height`.

I feel like this would be a good change for the Bootstrap framework as a whole.

However, this would be a breaking change, and I understand why you wouldn't want to do that.
author: pricop

title: Backdrop-blur Utility
body:
Glass effects using `backdrop-filter` are widely used in modern UI design. Happy to see this already in the v6 dialog/offcanvas backdrop. While Bootstrap provides the Utilities API to generate custom utilities, it may now be time to include a `backdrop-blur` utility by default.

I'm currently using this (v5), which results in a small CSS output:

Demo: https://codepen.io/craftwerkberlin/pen/bNwqLMN

```scss
// Add a custom backdrop-blur utility
$utilities: map-merge(
  $utilities,
  (
    "blur": (
      property: (
        backdrop-filter,
        -webkit-backdrop-filter
      ),
      class: bd-blur,
      values: (
        1: blur(5px) saturate(180%),
        2: blur(10px) saturate(180%),
        3: blur(15px) saturate(180%),
        4: blur(20px) saturate(180%),
        5: blur(25px) saturate(180%),
      )
    )
  )
);
```


This creates a new utility `bd-blur-{1-5}` that can be used in combination with other (v6) bg utilities (color, opacity), for example `bg-white fg-white bg-10 bd-blur-5`. It can be used in sticky navbars, card overlays, or anywhere one layer overlaps another.

If this is of interest, I’d be happy to fine-tune it and work on a PR. Let me know what you think.

title: Bootstrap UI Sounds
body:
I recently released Web DevTools+, an iOS app which brings desktop-class web developer tools to your iPad or iPhone. It includes a DOM element inspector, accessibility audits, network inspector, SEO tools, OpenGraph previews, console and much more. All the CSS used to create it is Bootstrap v5 :-)
https://apps.apple.com/us/app/web-devtools/id6759253731

In the app's settings I added a switch to turn on UI sounds (subtle effects when tapping buttons, switches, accordions etc) which I really liked and started to add as option in other projects.

I started this as a fun experiment but then thought others might be interested in it so I put a quick demo and code on GitHub to share. Every component that can be interacted with has a sound using the Web Audio API. I like that the range slider's volume changes depending on the value. (zero is silent and 100 is max). I think many of the sounds could be tweaked to be nicer and may work on that in a future update.

title: Combobox: List selected items with badges/input chips
body:
I really love the new combobox component, especially the search feature. I was really looking forward to replacing [use-bootstrap-select ](https://use-bootstrap-select.js.org/#/demo/)for some time, and this might be the opportunity.

A feature that is very popular all over the web is select boxes with multiple inputs and a badge-like appearance for the selected items in the closed select box—something that visually looks like the new input chips. I think it would be beneficial to merge the presentation of the input chips into the combobox. In the best case, this would also include the ability to add new items. I know the new-items feature is highly dependent on your backend, but it could perhaps just trigger an event. This could then be handled in JS per backend or maybe there is a general approach to this.

For reference:
On my Ruby on Rails backend, I currently handle this by submitting the new item (let’s call it “Orange”) in the id field (id=“Orange”).
I have not implemented this with multiple new items, though.

title: v6: Docs - feedback / bugs
body:
- **General: Sidebar** - I think the left nav sidebar is long and could benefit from having the sections collapsible by default, like Bootstrap 5.1 docs used to be (https://getbootstrap.com/docs/5.1/getting-started/introduction/). Or perhaps a filter at the top? Because the components are divided into sections and there is an increase in pages it can sometimes take a short while to find the page you're after. - **General: Info section at top of Pages**: have a new light gray background section for info and links but it's not clear which are links and which aren't. Thinking about it, overall it might make sense for these to be individual buttons or badges instead. Perhaps consider using the external link (diagonal arrow coming our of a square) for external links? I personally think it makes more sense to have the 'View & Edit this page on GitHub' at the bottom of the article too as many other docs sites do. - **General: Code** is in the same color as body text now. I preferred it as it was in v5 when it was pink, it's quicker to see class names at a quick glance.
Details v6 Docs: v5 Docs:
- **Search** - Site Search is super useful as there is an increase in pages, would be good to make the search input bigger on larger screens to increase the tap/click area. The border could do with a bit more contrast too probably... - **Homepage** - maybe remove the 'without jQuery' part from the JavaScript section header. It was relevant for v5, but not so much now as it's been so long since jQuery was used in Bootstrap. Also the 'Comprehensive set of plugins' needs updating or add a 'plus many more' at the bottom. - Stackblitz buttons missing from code examples ## Content - **Images** - https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/content/images/ The Navbar example has a horizontal grip to resize the example. Could be helpful to add it to the responsive image example too to see it resize. Bug? The SVGs have titles so they appear as tooltips on hover, maybe the titles can be removed since they have `aria-label`s anyway. (this issue is present in v5 docs too). ## Layout - **Grid** - The CSS-Tricks Flexbox guide is linked to both in the gray bar and as an info alert. I guess one of them can be removed. ## Forms - **Form Controls** - BUG: Date & Time section [appears](https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/forms/form-control/#date--time) [twice](https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/forms/form-control/#date-and-time). Date & Time inputs need d-block class I think so the icons are right aligned. ## Components - **Menu** - minor thing, but for completeness sake maybe the [icons example](https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/components/menu/#icons) should have a paste option and Cut should come above Copy. ## Overall - Overall the Docs site looks awesome.. I love the idea of making the docs template itself a separate open-source project for others to use... kind of like what Astro did with [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/). I understand the core teams resources are limited and it'd add a support burden on top (and a level of complexity if it was a separate repo) so might not be feasible though.

body:
Hi Bootstrap team, I've taken a spin through the v6 docs in progress, and I'm excited about a number of the updates and new features (though admittedly a bit daunted by the migration ahead). I discovered a number of UI issues in the docs, many of which are probably just a matter of needing to update the example code to match the new functionality, but it's possible some indicate underlying issues in components. I had started to try to fix a couple via a PR but quickly realized I wasn't familiar enough with the system and some things (like breakpoints) will need a broader refactor.

Should this section be removed?~~ (fixed by https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42356)

I see that the v5 docs achieve this via sass adding a 1rem top margin to `.bd-example > .progress + .progress` and I think the same could be done here.~~ (fixed by https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42344)

There are a number of similarly affected spots when I search the docs code, e.g. [responsive tables](https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/content/tables/#breakpoint-specific))~~ (tackled in https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/pull/42347)

author: coliff

title: Vanilla-Extract migration
body:
I am in the process of converting Bootstrap and Bootswatch themes from SCSS to Vanilla-Extract, using Solid v2.0 beta components to test.

it has a Playwright-driven screenshot pipeline: it renders every route/state/theme combination, generate the corresponding CSS artifacts, and compare screenshots as the source of truth to catch even tiny regressions. The code tackles real-world Bootstrap complexity (theme-scoped CSS variables, class-only styling constraints, tricky selector patterns, and Bootstrap JS overlays like popovers/modals that render outside the normal DOM subtree), and includes a custom override layer in a local Bootstrap JS fork to support safer class/selector customization.

The project is actively in progress and not complete yet, but it’s being built with a rigorous, testable migration strategy aimed at producing a maintainable, modern SolidJS + Bootstrap/Bootswatch codebase with reproducible visual validation.

See https://github.com/arijs/bootswatch-solid/blob/main/docs/ve-architecture.md

title: Change _start and _end back to _left and _right.
body:
I don't know why the change occurred, but can we please go back to left and right instead of start and end? The thinking is illogical now. I want left, not start. Text_left for left alignment, text_right for right alignment. Simple. Who changed it to start and end? And why? I upgraded from bootstrap 3.4 to 5 and had to change my entire project cuz someone thought start is better than left. When I want left! Not start!

Fix it and I'll be glad to change ALL my code again when the fixed version arrives. Please, sooner rather than later. And don't tell me this is better. Left should be called left. Simple. So many classes affected and for what? Cuz some noob didn't think that EVERYONE will have to change that now. All projects suddenly broken.

Fix it.

title: Introducing bootstrap_ui_flutter – A Flutter/Dart port of Bootstrap 5.3
body:
***Updated: v0.5.0 released – see comment below***
Hey Bootstrap community! 👋

I'm excited to share a project I've been working on: **bootstrap_ui_flutter** – a Flutter/Dart port of Bootstrap 5.3!

As a longtime Bootstrap user, I always missed the familiar Bootstrap aesthetic when building Flutter apps. So I decided to bring it to the Flutter world.

## What's already in there

- 🎨 **Design Tokens** – Bootstrap colors, typography, spacing, and breakpoints
- 📐 **Responsive Grid** – Full 12-column grid with `BsContainer`, `BsRow`, and `BsCol`
- 🧩 **Components** – Button, ButtonGroup, Alert, Badge, Accordion
- 🌙 **Dark/Light Mode** – Full native support for Bootstrap 5.3 dark theme

## Why I built this

Flutter is growing fast – especially on desktop and web. But there's no established Bootstrap-equivalent widget system for Flutter yet. I want to change that.

## What's coming next

This is just the beginning! I'll be continuously adding more Bootstrap components over time – staying as close to the original Bootstrap 5.3 spec as possible.

## Where to find it

- 📦 pub.dev: https://pub.dev/packages/bootstrap_ui_flutter
- 💻 GitHub: https://github.com/Nexus633/bootstrap_ui_flutter

I'd love to hear your thoughts! Is this something the Bootstrap community finds valuable? Any components you'd love to see next?

Looking forward to your feedback! 🚀
author: Nexus633

title: OTP component accessibility
body:
Documentation for this new component says 'Each input should have an aria-label describing its position (e.g., "Digit 1")'. I'm not at all an expert on assistive technology from an end-user perspective, but wouldn't this get really annoying if each label is read out for the user? It's probably too late to re-engineer this thing now, but thought I'd ask the question anyway. Our home-grown OTP component ends up looking something like this, so assistive technology users are dealing with a typical form input. Scripting puts the input from the big boxes into the standard input for submission and jumps the focus from item to item. ```html
```

title: v6: Dialog default styling
body: Was testing the Dialog component at https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/components/dialog/#example In the dark mode, I felt that the backdrop blur of 8px is a bit jarring on open/close, and when present, focusing on the dialog gave me an uneasy feeling. The dialog background color also seems too close to that of the backdrop's, so even after the blur, the dialog didn't seem to have a clear boundary. For easy comparison: Overall it seemed easier to switch focus in and out of v5 dialogs. Maybe it's just me, in which case I can update the config, but I've used many UI frameworks, and I don't really remember feeling.. woozy, so thought I should bring it up :)
